                          AREA ||i.uxTaskGetSystemState||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  uxTaskGetSystemState PROC
;;;2566   
;;;2567       UBaseType_t uxTaskGetSystemState( TaskStatus_t * const pxTaskStatusArray,
000000  e92d41f0          PUSH     {r4-r8,lr}
;;;2568                                         const UBaseType_t uxArraySize,
;;;2569                                         uint32_t * const pulTotalRunTime )
;;;2570       {
000004  4605              MOV      r5,r0
000006  4688              MOV      r8,r1
000008  4616              MOV      r6,r2
;;;2571           UBaseType_t uxTask = 0, uxQueue = configMAX_PRIORITIES;
00000a  2400              MOVS     r4,#0
00000c  270f              MOVS     r7,#0xf
;;;2572   
;;;2573           vTaskSuspendAll();
00000e  f7fffffe          BL       vTaskSuspendAll
;;;2574           {
;;;2575               /* Is there a space in the array for each task in the system? */
;;;2576               if( uxArraySize >= uxCurrentNumberOfTasks )
000012  4822              LDR      r0,|L19.156|
000014  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; uxCurrentNumberOfTasks
000016  4580              CMP      r8,r0
000018  d33a              BCC      |L19.144|
;;;2577               {
;;;2578                   /* Fill in an TaskStatus_t structure with information on each
;;;2579                    * task in the Ready state. */
;;;2580                   do
00001a  bf00              NOP      
                  |L19.28|
;;;2581                   {
;;;2582                       uxQueue--;
00001c  1e7f              SUBS     r7,r7,#1
;;;2583                       uxTask += prvListTasksWithinSingleList( &( pxTaskStatusArray[ uxTask ] ), &( pxReadyTasksLists[ uxQueue ] ), eReady );
00001e  eb070287          ADD      r2,r7,r7,LSL #2
000022  4b1f              LDR      r3,|L19.160|
000024  eb030182          ADD      r1,r3,r2,LSL #2
000028  eb0402c4          ADD      r2,r4,r4,LSL #3
00002c  eb050082          ADD      r0,r5,r2,LSL #2
000030  2201              MOVS     r2,#1
000032  f7fffffe          BL       prvListTasksWithinSingleList
000036  4404              ADD      r4,r4,r0
;;;2584                   } while( uxQueue > ( UBaseType_t ) tskIDLE_PRIORITY ); /*lint !e961 MISRA exception as the casts are only redundant for some ports. */
000038  2f00              CMP      r7,#0
00003a  d1ef              BNE      |L19.28|
;;;2585   
;;;2586                   /* Fill in an TaskStatus_t structure with information on each
;;;2587                    * task in the Blocked state. */
;;;2588                   uxTask += prvListTasksWithinSingleList( &( pxTaskStatusArray[ uxTask ] ), ( List_t * ) pxDelayedTaskList, eBlocked );
00003c  eb0401c4          ADD      r1,r4,r4,LSL #3
000040  eb050081          ADD      r0,r5,r1,LSL #2
000044  2202              MOVS     r2,#2
000046  4917              LDR      r1,|L19.164|
000048  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; pxDelayedTaskList
00004a  f7fffffe          BL       prvListTasksWithinSingleList
00004e  4404              ADD      r4,r4,r0
;;;2589                   uxTask += prvListTasksWithinSingleList( &( pxTaskStatusArray[ uxTask ] ), ( List_t * ) pxOverflowDelayedTaskList, eBlocked );
000050  eb0401c4          ADD      r1,r4,r4,LSL #3
000054  eb050081          ADD      r0,r5,r1,LSL #2
000058  2202              MOVS     r2,#2
00005a  4913              LDR      r1,|L19.168|
00005c  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; pxOverflowDelayedTaskList
00005e  f7fffffe          BL       prvListTasksWithinSingleList
000062  4404              ADD      r4,r4,r0
;;;2590   
;;;2591                   #if ( INCLUDE_vTaskDelete == 1 )
;;;2592                       {
;;;2593                           /* Fill in an TaskStatus_t structure with information on
;;;2594                            * each task that has been deleted but not yet cleaned up. */
;;;2595                           uxTask += prvListTasksWithinSingleList( &( pxTaskStatusArray[ uxTask ] ), &xTasksWaitingTermination, eDeleted );
000064  eb0401c4          ADD      r1,r4,r4,LSL #3
000068  eb050081          ADD      r0,r5,r1,LSL #2
00006c  2204              MOVS     r2,#4
00006e  490f              LDR      r1,|L19.172|
000070  f7fffffe          BL       prvListTasksWithinSingleList
000074  4404              ADD      r4,r4,r0
;;;2596                       }
;;;2597                   #endif
;;;2598   
;;;2599                   #if ( INCLUDE_vTaskSuspend == 1 )
;;;2600                       {
;;;2601                           /* Fill in an TaskStatus_t structure with information on
;;;2602                            * each task in the Suspended state. */
;;;2603                           uxTask += prvListTasksWithinSingleList( &( pxTaskStatusArray[ uxTask ] ), &xSuspendedTaskList, eSuspended );
000076  eb0401c4          ADD      r1,r4,r4,LSL #3
00007a  eb050081          ADD      r0,r5,r1,LSL #2
00007e  2203              MOVS     r2,#3
000080  490b              LDR      r1,|L19.176|
000082  f7fffffe          BL       prvListTasksWithinSingleList
000086  4404              ADD      r4,r4,r0
;;;2604                       }
;;;2605                   #endif
;;;2606   
;;;2607                   #if ( configGENERATE_RUN_TIME_STATS == 1 )
;;;2608                       {
;;;2609                           if( pulTotalRunTime != NULL )
000088  b116              CBZ      r6,|L19.144|
;;;2610                           {
;;;2611                               #ifdef portALT_GET_RUN_TIME_COUNTER_VALUE
;;;2612                                   portALT_GET_RUN_TIME_COUNTER_VALUE( ( *pulTotalRunTime ) );
;;;2613                               #else
;;;2614                                   *pulTotalRunTime = portGET_RUN_TIME_COUNTER_VALUE();
00008a  480a              LDR      r0,|L19.180|
00008c  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; ulHighFrequencyTimerTicks
00008e  6030              STR      r0,[r6,#0]
                  |L19.144|
;;;2615                               #endif
;;;2616                           }
;;;2617                       }
;;;2618                   #else /* if ( configGENERATE_RUN_TIME_STATS == 1 ) */
;;;2619                       {
;;;2620                           if( pulTotalRunTime != NULL )
;;;2621                           {
;;;2622                               *pulTotalRunTime = 0;
;;;2623                           }
;;;2624                       }
;;;2625                   #endif /* if ( configGENERATE_RUN_TIME_STATS == 1 ) */
;;;2626               }
;;;2627               else
;;;2628               {
;;;2629                   mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;2630               }
;;;2631           }
;;;2632           ( void ) xTaskResumeAll();
000090  f7fffffe          BL       xTaskResumeAll
;;;2633   
;;;2634           return uxTask;
000094  4620              MOV      r0,r4
;;;2635       }
000096  e8bd81f0          POP      {r4-r8,pc}
;;;2636   
                          ENDP

00009a  0000              DCW      0x0000
                  |L19.156|
                          DCD      uxCurrentNumberOfTasks
                  |L19.160|
                          DCD      pxReadyTasksLists
                  |L19.164|
                          DCD      pxDelayedTaskList
                  |L19.168|
                          DCD      pxOverflowDelayedTaskList
                  |L19.172|
                          DCD      xTasksWaitingTermination
                  |L19.176|
                          DCD      xSuspendedTaskList
                  |L19.180|
                          DCD      ulHighFrequencyTimerTicks

                          AREA ||i.uxTaskGetTaskNumber||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=1

                  uxTaskGetTaskNumber PROC
;;;3419   
;;;3420       UBaseType_t uxTaskGetTaskNumber( TaskHandle_t xTask )
000000  4601              MOV      r1,r0
;;;3421       {
;;;3422           UBaseType_t uxReturn;
;;;3423           TCB_t const * pxTCB;
;;;3424   
;;;3425           if( xTask != NULL )
000002  b111              CBZ      r1,|L20.10|
;;;3426           {
;;;3427               pxTCB = xTask;
000004  460a              MOV      r2,r1
;;;3428               uxReturn = pxTCB->uxTaskNumber;
000006  6c90              LDR      r0,[r2,#0x48]
000008  e000              B        |L20.12|
                  |L20.10|
;;;3429           }
;;;3430           else
;;;3431           {
;;;3432               uxReturn = 0U;
00000a  2000              MOVS     r0,#0
                  |L20.12|
;;;3433           }
;;;3434   
;;;3435           return uxReturn;
;;;3436       }
00000c  4770              BX       lr
;;;3437   
                          ENDP


                          AREA ||i.uxTaskPriorityGet||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  uxTaskPriorityGet PROC
;;;1522   
;;;1523       UBaseType_t uxTaskPriorityGet( const TaskHandle_t xTask )
000000  b570              PUSH     {r4-r6,lr}
;;;1524       {
000002  4604              MOV      r4,r0
;;;1525           TCB_t const * pxTCB;
;;;1526           UBaseType_t uxReturn;
;;;1527   
;;;1528           taskENTER_CRITICAL();
000004  f7fffffe          BL       vPortEnterCritical
;;;1529           {
;;;1530               /* If null is passed in here then it is the priority of the task
;;;1531                * that called uxTaskPriorityGet() that is being queried. */
;;;1532               pxTCB = prvGetTCBFromHandle( xTask );
000008  b914              CBNZ     r4,|L21.16|
00000a  4805              LDR      r0,|L21.32|
00000c  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
00000e  e000              B        |L21.18|
                  |L21.16|
000010  4620              MOV      r0,r4
                  |L21.18|
000012  4606              MOV      r6,r0
;;;1533               uxReturn = pxTCB->uxPriority;
000014  6af5              LDR      r5,[r6,#0x2c]
;;;1534           }
;;;1535           taskEXIT_CRITICAL();
000016  f7fffffe          BL       vPortExitCritical
;;;1536   
;;;1537           return uxReturn;
00001a  4628              MOV      r0,r5
;;;1538       }
00001c  bd70              POP      {r4-r6,pc}
;;;1539   
                          ENDP

00001e  0000              DCW      0x0000
                  |L21.32|
                          DCD      pxCurrentTCB

                          AREA ||i.uxTaskPriorityGetFromISR||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  uxTaskPriorityGetFromISR PROC
;;;1544   
;;;1545       UBaseType_t uxTaskPriorityGetFromISR( const TaskHandle_t xTask )
000000  b570              PUSH     {r4-r6,lr}
;;;1546       {
000002  4602              MOV      r2,r0
;;;1547           TCB_t const * pxTCB;
;;;1548           UBaseType_t uxReturn, uxSavedInterruptState;
;;;1549   
;;;1550           /* RTOS ports that support interrupt nesting have the concept of a
;;;1551            * maximum  system call (or maximum API call) interrupt priority.
;;;1552            * Interrupts that are  above the maximum system call priority are keep
;;;1553            * permanently enabled, even when the RTOS kernel is in a critical section,
;;;1554            * but cannot make any calls to FreeRTOS API functions.  If configASSERT()
;;;1555            * is defined in FreeRTOSConfig.h then
;;;1556            * portASSERT_IF_INTERRUPT_PRIORITY_INVALID() will result in an assertion
;;;1557            * failure if a FreeRTOS API function is called from an interrupt that has
;;;1558            * been assigned a priority above the configured maximum system call
;;;1559            * priority.  Only FreeRTOS functions that end in FromISR can be called
;;;1560            * from interrupts  that have been assigned a priority at or (logically)
;;;1561            * below the maximum system call interrupt priority.  FreeRTOS maintains a
;;;1562            * separate interrupt safe API to ensure interrupt entry is as fast and as
;;;1563            * simple as possible.  More information (albeit Cortex-M specific) is
;;;1564            * provided on the following link:
;;;1565            * https://www.FreeRTOS.org/RTOS-Cortex-M3-M4.html */
;;;1566           portASSERT_IF_INTERRUPT_PRIORITY_INVALID();
;;;1567   
;;;1568           uxSavedInterruptState = portSET_INTERRUPT_MASK_FROM_ISR();
000004  bf00              NOP      
000006  25bf              MOVS     r5,#0xbf
000008  f3ef8411          MRS      r4,BASEPRI
00000c  f3858811          MSR      BASEPRI,r5
000010  f3bf8f4f          DSB      
000014  f3bf8f6f          ISB      
000018  bf00              NOP      
00001a  4621              MOV      r1,r4
;;;1569           {
;;;1570               /* If null is passed in here then it is the priority of the calling
;;;1571                * task that is being queried. */
;;;1572               pxTCB = prvGetTCBFromHandle( xTask );
00001c  b912              CBNZ     r2,|L22.36|
00001e  4c05              LDR      r4,|L22.52|
000020  6824              LDR      r4,[r4,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000022  e000              B        |L22.38|
                  |L22.36|
000024  4614              MOV      r4,r2
                  |L22.38|
000026  4623              MOV      r3,r4
;;;1573               uxReturn = pxTCB->uxPriority;
000028  6ad8              LDR      r0,[r3,#0x2c]
;;;1574           }
;;;1575           portCLEAR_INTERRUPT_MASK_FROM_ISR( uxSavedInterruptState );
00002a  bf00              NOP      
00002c  f3818811          MSR      BASEPRI,r1
000030  bf00              NOP      
;;;1576   
;;;1577           return uxReturn;
;;;1578       }
000032  bd70              POP      {r4-r6,pc}
;;;1579   
                          ENDP

                  |L22.52|
                          DCD      pxCurrentTCB

                          AREA ||i.uxTaskResetEventItemValue||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  uxTaskResetEventItemValue PROC
;;;4695   
;;;4696   TickType_t uxTaskResetEventItemValue( void )
000000  4905              LDR      r1,|L23.24|
;;;4697   {
;;;4698       TickType_t uxReturn;
;;;4699   
;;;4700       uxReturn = listGET_LIST_ITEM_VALUE( &( pxCurrentTCB->xEventListItem ) );
000002  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000004  6988              LDR      r0,[r1,#0x18]
;;;4701   
;;;4702       /* Reset the event list item to its normal value - so it can be used with
;;;4703        * queues and semaphores. */
;;;4704       listSET_LIST_ITEM_VALUE( &( pxCurrentTCB->xEventListItem ), ( ( TickType_t ) configMAX_PRIORITIES - ( TickType_t ) pxCurrentTCB->uxPriority ) ); /*lint !e961 MISRA exception as the casts are only redundant for some ports. */
000006  4904              LDR      r1,|L23.24|
000008  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
00000a  6ac9              LDR      r1,[r1,#0x2c]
00000c  f1c1010f          RSB      r1,r1,#0xf
000010  4a01              LDR      r2,|L23.24|
000012  6812              LDR      r2,[r2,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000014  6191              STR      r1,[r2,#0x18]
;;;4705   
;;;4706       return uxReturn;
;;;4707   }
000016  4770              BX       lr
;;;4708   /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
                          ENDP

                  |L23.24|
                          DCD      pxCurrentTCB

                          AREA ||i.vTaskDelay||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  vTaskDelay PROC
;;;1380   
;;;1381       void vTaskDelay( const TickType_t xTicksToDelay )
000000  b570              PUSH     {r4-r6,lr}
;;;1382       {
000002  4604              MOV      r4,r0
;;;1383           BaseType_t xAlreadyYielded = pdFALSE;
000004  2500              MOVS     r5,#0
;;;1384   
;;;1385           /* A delay time of zero just forces a reschedule. */
;;;1386           if( xTicksToDelay > ( TickType_t ) 0U )
000006  b144              CBZ      r4,|L24.26|
;;;1387           {
;;;1388               configASSERT( uxSchedulerSuspended == 0 );
;;;1389               vTaskSuspendAll();
000008  f7fffffe          BL       vTaskSuspendAll
;;;1390               {
;;;1391                   traceTASK_DELAY();
;;;1392   
;;;1393                   /* A task that is removed from the event list while the
;;;1394                    * scheduler is suspended will not get placed in the ready
;;;1395                    * list or removed from the blocked list until the scheduler
;;;1396                    * is resumed.
;;;1397                    *
;;;1398                    * This task cannot be in an event list as it is the currently
;;;1399                    * executing task. */
;;;1400                   prvAddCurrentTaskToDelayedList( xTicksToDelay, pdFALSE );
00000c  2100              MOVS     r1,#0
00000e  4620              MOV      r0,r4
000010  f7fffffe          BL       prvAddCurrentTaskToDelayedList
;;;1401               }
;;;1402               xAlreadyYielded = xTaskResumeAll();
000014  f7fffffe          BL       xTaskResumeAll
000018  4605              MOV      r5,r0
                  |L24.26|
;;;1403           }
;;;1404           else
;;;1405           {
;;;1406               mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1407           }
;;;1408   
;;;1409           /* Force a reschedule if xTaskResumeAll has not already done so, we may
;;;1410            * have put ourselves to sleep. */
;;;1411           if( xAlreadyYielded == pdFALSE )
00001a  b93d              CBNZ     r5,|L24.44|
;;;1412           {
;;;1413               portYIELD_WITHIN_API();
00001c  f04f5080          MOV      r0,#0x10000000
000020  4903              LDR      r1,|L24.48|
000022  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]
000024  f3bf8f4f          DSB      
000028  f3bf8f6f          ISB      
                  |L24.44|
;;;1414           }
;;;1415           else
;;;1416           {
;;;1417               mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1418           }
;;;1419       }
00002c  bd70              POP      {r4-r6,pc}
;;;1420   
                          ENDP

00002e  0000              DCW      0x0000
                  |L24.48|
                          DCD      0xe000ed04

                          AREA ||i.vTaskDelete||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  vTaskDelete PROC
;;;1198   
;;;1199       void vTaskDelete( TaskHandle_t xTaskToDelete )
000000  b570              PUSH     {r4-r6,lr}
;;;1200       {
000002  4605              MOV      r5,r0
;;;1201           TCB_t * pxTCB;
;;;1202   
;;;1203           taskENTER_CRITICAL();
000004  f7fffffe          BL       vPortEnterCritical
;;;1204           {
;;;1205               /* If null is passed in here then it is the calling task that is
;;;1206                * being deleted. */
;;;1207               pxTCB = prvGetTCBFromHandle( xTaskToDelete );
000008  b915              CBNZ     r5,|L25.16|
00000a  4827              LDR      r0,|L25.168|
00000c  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
00000e  e000              B        |L25.18|
                  |L25.16|
000010  4628              MOV      r0,r5
                  |L25.18|
000012  4604              MOV      r4,r0
;;;1208   
;;;1209               /* Remove task from the ready/delayed list. */
;;;1210               if( uxListRemove( &( pxTCB->xStateListItem ) ) == ( UBaseType_t ) 0 )
000014  1d20              ADDS     r0,r4,#4
000016  f7fffffe          BL       uxListRemove
00001a  b978              CBNZ     r0,|L25.60|
;;;1211               {
;;;1212                   taskRESET_READY_PRIORITY( pxTCB->uxPriority );
00001c  6ae0              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x2c]
00001e  eb000080          ADD      r0,r0,r0,LSL #2
000022  4922              LDR      r1,|L25.172|
000024  f8510020          LDR      r0,[r1,r0,LSL #2]
000028  b940              CBNZ     r0,|L25.60|
00002a  f894102c          LDRB     r1,[r4,#0x2c]
00002e  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
000030  4088              LSLS     r0,r0,r1
000032  491f              LDR      r1,|L25.176|
000034  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
000036  4381              BICS     r1,r1,r0
000038  481d              LDR      r0,|L25.176|
00003a  6001              STR      r1,[r0,#0]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
                  |L25.60|
;;;1213               }
;;;1214               else
;;;1215               {
;;;1216                   mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1217               }
;;;1218   
;;;1219               /* Is the task waiting on an event also? */
;;;1220               if( listLIST_ITEM_CONTAINER( &( pxTCB->xEventListItem ) ) != NULL )
00003c  6aa0              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x28]
00003e  b118              CBZ      r0,|L25.72|
;;;1221               {
;;;1222                   ( void ) uxListRemove( &( pxTCB->xEventListItem ) );
000040  f1040018          ADD      r0,r4,#0x18
000044  f7fffffe          BL       uxListRemove
                  |L25.72|
;;;1223               }
;;;1224               else
;;;1225               {
;;;1226                   mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1227               }
;;;1228   
;;;1229               /* Increment the uxTaskNumber also so kernel aware debuggers can
;;;1230                * detect that the task lists need re-generating.  This is done before
;;;1231                * portPRE_TASK_DELETE_HOOK() as in the Windows port that macro will
;;;1232                * not return. */
;;;1233               uxTaskNumber++;
000048  481a              LDR      r0,|L25.180|
00004a  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; uxTaskNumber
00004c  1c40              ADDS     r0,r0,#1
00004e  4919              LDR      r1,|L25.180|
000050  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; uxTaskNumber
;;;1234   
;;;1235               if( pxTCB == pxCurrentTCB )
000052  4815              LDR      r0,|L25.168|
000054  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000056  4284              CMP      r4,r0
000058  d109              BNE      |L25.110|
;;;1236               {
;;;1237                   /* A task is deleting itself.  This cannot complete within the
;;;1238                    * task itself, as a context switch to another task is required.
;;;1239                    * Place the task in the termination list.  The idle task will
;;;1240                    * check the termination list and free up any memory allocated by
;;;1241                    * the scheduler for the TCB and stack of the deleted task. */
;;;1242                   vListInsertEnd( &xTasksWaitingTermination, &( pxTCB->xStateListItem ) );
00005a  1d21              ADDS     r1,r4,#4
00005c  4816              LDR      r0,|L25.184|
00005e  f7fffffe          BL       vListInsertEnd
;;;1243   
;;;1244                   /* Increment the ucTasksDeleted variable so the idle task knows
;;;1245                    * there is a task that has been deleted and that it should therefore
;;;1246                    * check the xTasksWaitingTermination list. */
;;;1247                   ++uxDeletedTasksWaitingCleanUp;
000062  4816              LDR      r0,|L25.188|
000064  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; uxDeletedTasksWaitingCleanUp
000066  1c40              ADDS     r0,r0,#1
000068  4914              LDR      r1,|L25.188|
00006a  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; uxDeletedTasksWaitingCleanUp
00006c  e009              B        |L25.130|
                  |L25.110|
;;;1248   
;;;1249                   /* Call the delete hook before portPRE_TASK_DELETE_HOOK() as
;;;1250                    * portPRE_TASK_DELETE_HOOK() does not return in the Win32 port. */
;;;1251                   traceTASK_DELETE( pxTCB );
;;;1252   
;;;1253                   /* The pre-delete hook is primarily for the Windows simulator,
;;;1254                    * in which Windows specific clean up operations are performed,
;;;1255                    * after which it is not possible to yield away from this task -
;;;1256                    * hence xYieldPending is used to latch that a context switch is
;;;1257                    * required. */
;;;1258                   portPRE_TASK_DELETE_HOOK( pxTCB, &xYieldPending );
;;;1259               }
;;;1260               else
;;;1261               {
;;;1262                   --uxCurrentNumberOfTasks;
00006e  4814              LDR      r0,|L25.192|
000070  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; uxCurrentNumberOfTasks
000072  1e40              SUBS     r0,r0,#1
000074  4912              LDR      r1,|L25.192|
000076  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; uxCurrentNumberOfTasks
;;;1263                   traceTASK_DELETE( pxTCB );
;;;1264                   prvDeleteTCB( pxTCB );
000078  4620              MOV      r0,r4
00007a  f7fffffe          BL       prvDeleteTCB
;;;1265   
;;;1266                   /* Reset the next expected unblock time in case it referred to
;;;1267                    * the task that has just been deleted. */
;;;1268                   prvResetNextTaskUnblockTime();
00007e  f7fffffe          BL       prvResetNextTaskUnblockTime
                  |L25.130|
;;;1269               }
;;;1270           }
;;;1271           taskEXIT_CRITICAL();
000082  f7fffffe          BL       vPortExitCritical
;;;1272   
;;;1273           /* Force a reschedule if it is the currently running task that has just
;;;1274            * been deleted. */
;;;1275           if( xSchedulerRunning != pdFALSE )
000086  480f              LDR      r0,|L25.196|
000088  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; xSchedulerRunning
00008a  b158              CBZ      r0,|L25.164|
;;;1276           {
;;;1277               if( pxTCB == pxCurrentTCB )
00008c  4806              LDR      r0,|L25.168|
00008e  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000090  4284              CMP      r4,r0
000092  d107              BNE      |L25.164|
;;;1278               {
;;;1279                   configASSERT( uxSchedulerSuspended == 0 );
;;;1280                   portYIELD_WITHIN_API();
000094  f04f5080          MOV      r0,#0x10000000
000098  490b              LDR      r1,|L25.200|
00009a  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]
00009c  f3bf8f4f          DSB      
0000a0  f3bf8f6f          ISB      
                  |L25.164|
;;;1281               }
;;;1282               else
;;;1283               {
;;;1284                   mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1285               }
;;;1286           }
;;;1287       }
0000a4  bd70              POP      {r4-r6,pc}
;;;1288   
                          ENDP

0000a6  0000              DCW      0x0000
                  |L25.168|
                          DCD      pxCurrentTCB
                  |L25.172|
                          DCD      pxReadyTasksLists
                  |L25.176|
                          DCD      uxTopReadyPriority
                  |L25.180|
                          DCD      uxTaskNumber
                  |L25.184|
                          DCD      xTasksWaitingTermination
                  |L25.188|
                          DCD      uxDeletedTasksWaitingCleanUp
                  |L25.192|
                          DCD      uxCurrentNumberOfTasks
                  |L25.196|
                          DCD      xSchedulerRunning
                  |L25.200|
                          DCD      0xe000ed04

                          AREA ||i.vTaskEndScheduler||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  vTaskEndScheduler PROC
;;;2159   
;;;2160   void vTaskEndScheduler( void )
000000  b510              PUSH     {r4,lr}
;;;2161   {
;;;2162       /* Stop the scheduler interrupts and call the portable scheduler end
;;;2163        * routine so the original ISRs can be restored if necessary.  The port
;;;2164        * layer must ensure interrupts enable  bit is left in the correct state. */
;;;2165       portDISABLE_INTERRUPTS();
000002  bf00              NOP      
000004  20bf              MOVS     r0,#0xbf
000006  f3808811          MSR      BASEPRI,r0
00000a  f3bf8f4f          DSB      
00000e  f3bf8f6f          ISB      
000012  bf00              NOP      
;;;2166       xSchedulerRunning = pdFALSE;
000014  2000              MOVS     r0,#0
000016  4902              LDR      r1,|L26.32|
000018  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; xSchedulerRunning
;;;2167       vPortEndScheduler();
00001a  f7fffffe          BL       vPortEndScheduler
;;;2168   }
00001e  bd10              POP      {r4,pc}
;;;2169   /*----------------------------------------------------------*/
                          ENDP

                  |L26.32|
                          DCD      xSchedulerRunning

                          AREA ||i.vTaskGenericNotifyGiveFromISR||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  vTaskGenericNotifyGiveFromISR PROC
;;;5148   
;;;5149       void vTaskGenericNotifyGiveFromISR( TaskHandle_t xTaskToNotify,
000000  e92d47f0          PUSH     {r4-r10,lr}
;;;5150                                           UBaseType_t uxIndexToNotify,
;;;5151                                           BaseType_t * pxHigherPriorityTaskWoken )
;;;5152       {
000004  4605              MOV      r5,r0
000006  460e              MOV      r6,r1
000008  4617              MOV      r7,r2
;;;5153           TCB_t * pxTCB;
;;;5154           uint8_t ucOriginalNotifyState;
;;;5155           UBaseType_t uxSavedInterruptStatus;
;;;5156   
;;;5157           configASSERT( xTaskToNotify );
;;;5158           configASSERT( uxIndexToNotify < configTASK_NOTIFICATION_ARRAY_ENTRIES );
;;;5159   
;;;5160           /* RTOS ports that support interrupt nesting have the concept of a
;;;5161            * maximum  system call (or maximum API call) interrupt priority.
;;;5162            * Interrupts that are  above the maximum system call priority are keep
;;;5163            * permanently enabled, even when the RTOS kernel is in a critical section,
;;;5164            * but cannot make any calls to FreeRTOS API functions.  If configASSERT()
;;;5165            * is defined in FreeRTOSConfig.h then
;;;5166            * portASSERT_IF_INTERRUPT_PRIORITY_INVALID() will result in an assertion
;;;5167            * failure if a FreeRTOS API function is called from an interrupt that has
;;;5168            * been assigned a priority above the configured maximum system call
;;;5169            * priority.  Only FreeRTOS functions that end in FromISR can be called
;;;5170            * from interrupts  that have been assigned a priority at or (logically)
;;;5171            * below the maximum system call interrupt priority.  FreeRTOS maintains a
;;;5172            * separate interrupt safe API to ensure interrupt entry is as fast and as
;;;5173            * simple as possible.  More information (albeit Cortex-M specific) is
;;;5174            * provided on the following link:
;;;5175            * https://www.FreeRTOS.org/RTOS-Cortex-M3-M4.html */
;;;5176           portASSERT_IF_INTERRUPT_PRIORITY_INVALID();
;;;5177   
;;;5178           pxTCB = xTaskToNotify;
00000a  462c              MOV      r4,r5
;;;5179   
;;;5180           uxSavedInterruptStatus = portSET_INTERRUPT_MASK_FROM_ISR();
00000c  bf00              NOP      
00000e  21bf              MOVS     r1,#0xbf
000010  f3ef8011          MRS      r0,BASEPRI
000014  f3818811          MSR      BASEPRI,r1
000018  f3bf8f4f          DSB      
00001c  f3bf8f6f          ISB      
000020  bf00              NOP      
000022  4680              MOV      r8,r0
;;;5181           {
;;;5182               ucOriginalNotifyState = pxTCB->ucNotifyState[ uxIndexToNotify ];
000024  f104005c          ADD      r0,r4,#0x5c
000028  f8109006          LDRB     r9,[r0,r6]
;;;5183               pxTCB->ucNotifyState[ uxIndexToNotify ] = taskNOTIFICATION_RECEIVED;
00002c  2102              MOVS     r1,#2
00002e  5581              STRB     r1,[r0,r6]
;;;5184   
;;;5185               /* 'Giving' is equivalent to incrementing a count in a counting
;;;5186                * semaphore. */
;;;5187               ( pxTCB->ulNotifiedValue[ uxIndexToNotify ] )++;
000030  1f00              SUBS     r0,r0,#4
000032  f8500026          LDR      r0,[r0,r6,LSL #2]
000036  1c40              ADDS     r0,r0,#1
000038  f1040158          ADD      r1,r4,#0x58
00003c  f8410026          STR      r0,[r1,r6,LSL #2]
;;;5188   
;;;5189               traceTASK_NOTIFY_GIVE_FROM_ISR( uxIndexToNotify );
;;;5190   
;;;5191               /* If the task is in the blocked state specifically to wait for a
;;;5192                * notification then unblock it now. */
;;;5193               if( ucOriginalNotifyState == taskWAITING_NOTIFICATION )
000040  f1b90f01          CMP      r9,#1
000044  d129              BNE      |L27.154|
;;;5194               {
;;;5195                   /* The task should not have been on an event list. */
;;;5196                   configASSERT( listLIST_ITEM_CONTAINER( &( pxTCB->xEventListItem ) ) == NULL );
;;;5197   
;;;5198                   if( uxSchedulerSuspended == ( UBaseType_t ) pdFALSE )
000046  4818              LDR      r0,|L27.168|
000048  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; uxSchedulerSuspended
00004a  b9a8              CBNZ     r0,|L27.120|
;;;5199                   {
;;;5200                       ( void ) uxListRemove( &( pxTCB->xStateListItem ) );
00004c  1d20              ADDS     r0,r4,#4
00004e  f7fffffe          BL       uxListRemove
;;;5201                       prvAddTaskToReadyList( pxTCB );
000052  f894102c          LDRB     r1,[r4,#0x2c]
000056  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
000058  4088              LSLS     r0,r0,r1
00005a  4914              LDR      r1,|L27.172|
00005c  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
00005e  4308              ORRS     r0,r0,r1
000060  4912              LDR      r1,|L27.172|
000062  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
000064  6ae1              LDR      r1,[r4,#0x2c]
000066  eb010181          ADD      r1,r1,r1,LSL #2
00006a  4a11              LDR      r2,|L27.176|
00006c  eb020081          ADD      r0,r2,r1,LSL #2
000070  1d21              ADDS     r1,r4,#4
000072  f7fffffe          BL       vListInsertEnd
000076  e004              B        |L27.130|
                  |L27.120|
;;;5202                   }
;;;5203                   else
;;;5204                   {
;;;5205                       /* The delayed and ready lists cannot be accessed, so hold
;;;5206                        * this task pending until the scheduler is resumed. */
;;;5207                       vListInsertEnd( &( xPendingReadyList ), &( pxTCB->xEventListItem ) );
000078  f1040118          ADD      r1,r4,#0x18
00007c  480d              LDR      r0,|L27.180|
00007e  f7fffffe          BL       vListInsertEnd
                  |L27.130|
;;;5208                   }
;;;5209   
;;;5210                   if( pxTCB->uxPriority > pxCurrentTCB->uxPriority )
000082  490d              LDR      r1,|L27.184|
000084  6ae0              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x2c]
000086  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000088  6ac9              LDR      r1,[r1,#0x2c]
00008a  4288              CMP      r0,r1
00008c  d905              BLS      |L27.154|
;;;5211                   {
;;;5212                       /* The notified task has a priority above the currently
;;;5213                        * executing task so a yield is required. */
;;;5214                       if( pxHigherPriorityTaskWoken != NULL )
00008e  b10f              CBZ      r7,|L27.148|
;;;5215                       {
;;;5216                           *pxHigherPriorityTaskWoken = pdTRUE;
000090  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
000092  6038              STR      r0,[r7,#0]
                  |L27.148|
;;;5217                       }
;;;5218   
;;;5219                       /* Mark that a yield is pending in case the user is not
;;;5220                        * using the "xHigherPriorityTaskWoken" parameter in an ISR
;;;5221                        * safe FreeRTOS function. */
;;;5222                       xYieldPending = pdTRUE;
000094  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
000096  4909              LDR      r1,|L27.188|
000098  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; xYieldPending
                  |L27.154|
;;;5223                   }
;;;5224                   else
;;;5225                   {
;;;5226                       mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;5227                   }
;;;5228               }
;;;5229           }
;;;5230           portCLEAR_INTERRUPT_MASK_FROM_ISR( uxSavedInterruptStatus );
00009a  bf00              NOP      
00009c  f3888811          MSR      BASEPRI,r8
0000a0  bf00              NOP      
;;;5231       }
0000a2  e8bd87f0          POP      {r4-r10,pc}
;;;5232   
                          ENDP

0000a6  0000              DCW      0x0000
                  |L27.168|
                          DCD      uxSchedulerSuspended
                  |L27.172|
                          DCD      uxTopReadyPriority
                  |L27.176|
                          DCD      pxReadyTasksLists
                  |L27.180|
                          DCD      xPendingReadyList
                  |L27.184|
                          DCD      pxCurrentTCB
                  |L27.188|
                          DCD      xYieldPending

                          AREA ||i.vTaskGetInfo||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  vTaskGetInfo PROC
;;;3754   
;;;3755       void vTaskGetInfo( TaskHandle_t xTask,
000000  e92d41f0          PUSH     {r4-r8,lr}
;;;3756                          TaskStatus_t * pxTaskStatus,
;;;3757                          BaseType_t xGetFreeStackSpace,
;;;3758                          eTaskState eState )
;;;3759       {
000004  4607              MOV      r7,r0
000006  460c              MOV      r4,r1
000008  4690              MOV      r8,r2
00000a  461e              MOV      r6,r3
;;;3760           TCB_t * pxTCB;
;;;3761   
;;;3762           /* xTask is NULL then get the state of the calling task. */
;;;3763           pxTCB = prvGetTCBFromHandle( xTask );
00000c  b917              CBNZ     r7,|L28.20|
00000e  481c              LDR      r0,|L28.128|
000010  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000012  e000              B        |L28.22|
                  |L28.20|
000014  4638              MOV      r0,r7
                  |L28.22|
000016  4605              MOV      r5,r0
;;;3764   
;;;3765           pxTaskStatus->xHandle = ( TaskHandle_t ) pxTCB;
000018  6025              STR      r5,[r4,#0]
;;;3766           pxTaskStatus->pcTaskName = ( const char * ) &( pxTCB->pcTaskName[ 0 ] );
00001a  f1050034          ADD      r0,r5,#0x34
00001e  6060              STR      r0,[r4,#4]
;;;3767           pxTaskStatus->uxCurrentPriority = pxTCB->uxPriority;
000020  6ae8              LDR      r0,[r5,#0x2c]
000022  6120              STR      r0,[r4,#0x10]
;;;3768           pxTaskStatus->pxStackBase = pxTCB->pxStack;
000024  6b28              LDR      r0,[r5,#0x30]
000026  61e0              STR      r0,[r4,#0x1c]
;;;3769           pxTaskStatus->xTaskNumber = pxTCB->uxTCBNumber;
000028  6c68              LDR      r0,[r5,#0x44]
00002a  60a0              STR      r0,[r4,#8]
;;;3770   
;;;3771           #if ( configUSE_MUTEXES == 1 )
;;;3772               {
;;;3773                   pxTaskStatus->uxBasePriority = pxTCB->uxBasePriority;
00002c  6ce8              LDR      r0,[r5,#0x4c]
00002e  6160              STR      r0,[r4,#0x14]
;;;3774               }
;;;3775           #else
;;;3776               {
;;;3777                   pxTaskStatus->uxBasePriority = 0;
;;;3778               }
;;;3779           #endif
;;;3780   
;;;3781           #if ( configGENERATE_RUN_TIME_STATS == 1 )
;;;3782               {
;;;3783                   pxTaskStatus->ulRunTimeCounter = pxTCB->ulRunTimeCounter;
000030  6d68              LDR      r0,[r5,#0x54]
000032  61a0              STR      r0,[r4,#0x18]
;;;3784               }
;;;3785           #else
;;;3786               {
;;;3787                   pxTaskStatus->ulRunTimeCounter = 0;
;;;3788               }
;;;3789           #endif
;;;3790   
;;;3791           /* Obtaining the task state is a little fiddly, so is only done if the
;;;3792            * value of eState passed into this function is eInvalid - otherwise the
;;;3793            * state is just set to whatever is passed in. */
;;;3794           if( eState != eInvalid )
000034  2e05              CMP      r6,#5
000036  d012              BEQ      |L28.94|
;;;3795           {
;;;3796               if( pxTCB == pxCurrentTCB )
000038  4811              LDR      r0,|L28.128|
00003a  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
00003c  4285              CMP      r5,r0
00003e  d102              BNE      |L28.70|
;;;3797               {
;;;3798                   pxTaskStatus->eCurrentState = eRunning;
000040  2000              MOVS     r0,#0
000042  7320              STRB     r0,[r4,#0xc]
000044  e00f              B        |L28.102|
                  |L28.70|
;;;3799               }
;;;3800               else
;;;3801               {
;;;3802                   pxTaskStatus->eCurrentState = eState;
000046  7326              STRB     r6,[r4,#0xc]
;;;3803   
;;;3804                   #if ( INCLUDE_vTaskSuspend == 1 )
;;;3805                       {
;;;3806                           /* If the task is in the suspended list then there is a
;;;3807                            *  chance it is actually just blocked indefinitely - so really
;;;3808                            *  it should be reported as being in the Blocked state. */
;;;3809                           if( eState == eSuspended )
000048  2e03              CMP      r6,#3
00004a  d10c              BNE      |L28.102|
;;;3810                           {
;;;3811                               vTaskSuspendAll();
00004c  f7fffffe          BL       vTaskSuspendAll
;;;3812                               {
;;;3813                                   if( listLIST_ITEM_CONTAINER( &( pxTCB->xEventListItem ) ) != NULL )
000050  6aa8              LDR      r0,[r5,#0x28]
000052  b108              CBZ      r0,|L28.88|
;;;3814                                   {
;;;3815                                       pxTaskStatus->eCurrentState = eBlocked;
000054  2002              MOVS     r0,#2
000056  7320              STRB     r0,[r4,#0xc]
                  |L28.88|
;;;3816                                   }
;;;3817                               }
;;;3818                               ( void ) xTaskResumeAll();
000058  f7fffffe          BL       xTaskResumeAll
00005c  e003              B        |L28.102|
                  |L28.94|
;;;3819                           }
;;;3820                       }
;;;3821                   #endif /* INCLUDE_vTaskSuspend */
;;;3822               }
;;;3823           }
;;;3824           else
;;;3825           {
;;;3826               pxTaskStatus->eCurrentState = eTaskGetState( pxTCB );
00005e  4628              MOV      r0,r5
000060  f7fffffe          BL       eTaskGetState
000064  7320              STRB     r0,[r4,#0xc]
                  |L28.102|
;;;3827           }
;;;3828   
;;;3829           /* Obtaining the stack space takes some time, so the xGetFreeStackSpace
;;;3830            * parameter is provided to allow it to be skipped. */
;;;3831           if( xGetFreeStackSpace != pdFALSE )
000066  f1b80f00          CMP      r8,#0
00006a  d004              BEQ      |L28.118|
;;;3832           {
;;;3833               #if ( portSTACK_GROWTH > 0 )
;;;3834                   {
;;;3835                       pxTaskStatus->usStackHighWaterMark = prvTaskCheckFreeStackSpace( ( uint8_t * ) pxTCB->pxEndOfStack );
;;;3836                   }
;;;3837               #else
;;;3838                   {
;;;3839                       pxTaskStatus->usStackHighWaterMark = prvTaskCheckFreeStackSpace( ( uint8_t * ) pxTCB->pxStack );
00006c  6b28              LDR      r0,[r5,#0x30]
00006e  f7fffffe          BL       prvTaskCheckFreeStackSpace
000072  8420              STRH     r0,[r4,#0x20]
000074  e001              B        |L28.122|
                  |L28.118|
;;;3840                   }
;;;3841               #endif
;;;3842           }
;;;3843           else
;;;3844           {
;;;3845               pxTaskStatus->usStackHighWaterMark = 0;
000076  2000              MOVS     r0,#0
000078  8420              STRH     r0,[r4,#0x20]
                  |L28.122|
;;;3846           }
;;;3847       }
00007a  e8bd81f0          POP      {r4-r8,pc}
;;;3848   
                          ENDP

00007e  0000              DCW      0x0000
                  |L28.128|
                          DCD      pxCurrentTCB

                          AREA ||i.vTaskGetRunTimeStats||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                          REQUIRE _printf_percent
                          REQUIRE _printf_u
                          REQUIRE _printf_int_dec
                  vTaskGetRunTimeStats PROC
;;;4570   
;;;4571       void vTaskGetRunTimeStats( char * pcWriteBuffer )
000000  e92d43f8          PUSH     {r3-r9,lr}
;;;4572       {
000004  4606              MOV      r6,r0
;;;4573           TaskStatus_t * pxTaskStatusArray;
;;;4574           UBaseType_t uxArraySize, x;
;;;4575           uint32_t ulTotalTime, ulStatsAsPercentage;
;;;4576   
;;;4577           #if ( configUSE_TRACE_FACILITY != 1 )
;;;4578               {
;;;4579                   #error configUSE_TRACE_FACILITY must also be set to 1 in FreeRTOSConfig.h to use vTaskGetRunTimeStats().
;;;4580               }
;;;4581           #endif
;;;4582   
;;;4583           /*
;;;4584            * PLEASE NOTE:
;;;4585            *
;;;4586            * This function is provided for convenience only, and is used by many
;;;4587            * of the demo applications.  Do not consider it to be part of the
;;;4588            * scheduler.
;;;4589            *
;;;4590            * vTaskGetRunTimeStats() calls uxTaskGetSystemState(), then formats part
;;;4591            * of the uxTaskGetSystemState() output into a human readable table that
;;;4592            * displays the amount of time each task has spent in the Running state
;;;4593            * in both absolute and percentage terms.
;;;4594            *
;;;4595            * vTaskGetRunTimeStats() has a dependency on the sprintf() C library
;;;4596            * function that might bloat the code size, use a lot of stack, and
;;;4597            * provide different results on different platforms.  An alternative,
;;;4598            * tiny, third party, and limited functionality implementation of
;;;4599            * sprintf() is provided in many of the FreeRTOS/Demo sub-directories in
;;;4600            * a file called printf-stdarg.c (note printf-stdarg.c does not provide
;;;4601            * a full snprintf() implementation!).
;;;4602            *
;;;4603            * It is recommended that production systems call uxTaskGetSystemState()
;;;4604            * directly to get access to raw stats data, rather than indirectly
;;;4605            * through a call to vTaskGetRunTimeStats().
;;;4606            */
;;;4607   
;;;4608           /* Make sure the write buffer does not contain a string. */
;;;4609           *pcWriteBuffer = ( char ) 0x00;
000006  2000              MOVS     r0,#0
000008  7030              STRB     r0,[r6,#0]
;;;4610   
;;;4611           /* Take a snapshot of the number of tasks in case it changes while this
;;;4612            * function is executing. */
;;;4613           uxArraySize = uxCurrentNumberOfTasks;
00000a  4827              LDR      r0,|L29.168|
00000c  f8d08000          LDR      r8,[r0,#0]  ; uxCurrentNumberOfTasks
;;;4614   
;;;4615           /* Allocate an array index for each task.  NOTE!  If
;;;4616            * configSUPPORT_DYNAMIC_ALLOCATION is set to 0 then pvPortMalloc() will
;;;4617            * equate to NULL. */
;;;4618           pxTaskStatusArray = pvPortMalloc( uxCurrentNumberOfTasks * sizeof( TaskStatus_t ) ); /*lint !e9079 All values returned by pvPortMalloc() have at least the alignment required by the MCU's stack and this allocation allocates a struct that has the alignment requirements of a pointer. */
000010  4601              MOV      r1,r0
000012  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; uxCurrentNumberOfTasks
000014  eb0101c1          ADD      r1,r1,r1,LSL #3
000018  0088              LSLS     r0,r1,#2
00001a  f7fffffe          BL       pvPortMalloc
00001e  4605              MOV      r5,r0
;;;4619   
;;;4620           if( pxTaskStatusArray != NULL )
000020  2d00              CMP      r5,#0
000022  d03f              BEQ      |L29.164|
;;;4621           {
;;;4622               /* Generate the (binary) data. */
;;;4623               uxArraySize = uxTaskGetSystemState( pxTaskStatusArray, uxArraySize, &ulTotalTime );
000024  466a              MOV      r2,sp
000026  4641              MOV      r1,r8
000028  4628              MOV      r0,r5
00002a  f7fffffe          BL       uxTaskGetSystemState
00002e  4680              MOV      r8,r0
;;;4624   
;;;4625               /* For percentage calculations. */
;;;4626               ulTotalTime /= 100UL;
000030  2164              MOVS     r1,#0x64
000032  9800              LDR      r0,[sp,#0]
000034  fbb0f0f1          UDIV     r0,r0,r1
000038  9000              STR      r0,[sp,#0]
;;;4627   
;;;4628               /* Avoid divide by zero errors. */
;;;4629               if( ulTotalTime > 0UL )
00003a  9800              LDR      r0,[sp,#0]
00003c  2800              CMP      r0,#0
00003e  d02e              BEQ      |L29.158|
;;;4630               {
;;;4631                   /* Create a human readable table from the binary data. */
;;;4632                   for( x = 0; x < uxArraySize; x++ )
000040  2400              MOVS     r4,#0
000042  e02a              B        |L29.154|
                  |L29.68|
;;;4633                   {
;;;4634                       /* What percentage of the total run time has the task used?
;;;4635                        * This will always be rounded down to the nearest integer.
;;;4636                        * ulTotalRunTimeDiv100 has already been divided by 100. */
;;;4637                       ulStatsAsPercentage = pxTaskStatusArray[ x ].ulRunTimeCounter / ulTotalTime;
000044  eb0400c4          ADD      r0,r4,r4,LSL #3
000048  eb050080          ADD      r0,r5,r0,LSL #2
00004c  6980              LDR      r0,[r0,#0x18]
00004e  9900              LDR      r1,[sp,#0]
000050  fbb0f7f1          UDIV     r7,r0,r1
;;;4638   
;;;4639                       /* Write the task name to the string, padding with
;;;4640                        * spaces so it can be printed in tabular form more
;;;4641                        * easily. */
;;;4642                       pcWriteBuffer = prvWriteNameToBuffer( pcWriteBuffer, pxTaskStatusArray[ x ].pcTaskName );
000054  eb0400c4          ADD      r0,r4,r4,LSL #3
000058  eb050080          ADD      r0,r5,r0,LSL #2
00005c  6841              LDR      r1,[r0,#4]
00005e  4630              MOV      r0,r6
000060  f7fffffe          BL       prvWriteNameToBuffer
000064  4606              MOV      r6,r0
;;;4643   
;;;4644                       if( ulStatsAsPercentage > 0UL )
000066  b157              CBZ      r7,|L29.126|
;;;4645                       {
;;;4646                           #ifdef portLU_PRINTF_SPECIFIER_REQUIRED
;;;4647                               {
;;;4648                                   sprintf( pcWriteBuffer, "\t%lu\t\t%lu%%\r\n", pxTaskStatusArray[ x ].ulRunTimeCounter, ulStatsAsPercentage );
;;;4649                               }
;;;4650                           #else
;;;4651                               {
;;;4652                                   /* sizeof( int ) == sizeof( long ) so a smaller
;;;4653                                    * printf() library can be used. */
;;;4654                                   sprintf( pcWriteBuffer, "\t%u\t\t%u%%\r\n", ( unsigned int ) pxTaskStatusArray[ x ].ulRunTimeCounter, ( unsigned int ) ulStatsAsPercentage ); /*lint !e586 sprintf() allowed as this is compiled with many compilers and this is a utility function only - not part of the core kernel implementation. */
000068  eb0400c4          ADD      r0,r4,r4,LSL #3
00006c  eb050080          ADD      r0,r5,r0,LSL #2
000070  463b              MOV      r3,r7
000072  a10e              ADR      r1,|L29.172|
000074  6982              LDR      r2,[r0,#0x18]
000076  4630              MOV      r0,r6
000078  f7fffffe          BL       __2sprintf
00007c  e008              B        |L29.144|
                  |L29.126|
;;;4655                               }
;;;4656                           #endif
;;;4657                       }
;;;4658                       else
;;;4659                       {
;;;4660                           /* If the percentage is zero here then the task has
;;;4661                            * consumed less than 1% of the total run time. */
;;;4662                           #ifdef portLU_PRINTF_SPECIFIER_REQUIRED
;;;4663                               {
;;;4664                                   sprintf( pcWriteBuffer, "\t%lu\t\t<1%%\r\n", pxTaskStatusArray[ x ].ulRunTimeCounter );
;;;4665                               }
;;;4666                           #else
;;;4667                               {
;;;4668                                   /* sizeof( int ) == sizeof( long ) so a smaller
;;;4669                                    * printf() library can be used. */
;;;4670                                   sprintf( pcWriteBuffer, "\t%u\t\t<1%%\r\n", ( unsigned int ) pxTaskStatusArray[ x ].ulRunTimeCounter ); /*lint !e586 sprintf() allowed as this is compiled with many compilers and this is a utility function only - not part of the core kernel implementation. */
00007e  eb0400c4          ADD      r0,r4,r4,LSL #3
000082  eb050080          ADD      r0,r5,r0,LSL #2
000086  a10c              ADR      r1,|L29.184|
000088  6982              LDR      r2,[r0,#0x18]
00008a  4630              MOV      r0,r6
00008c  f7fffffe          BL       __2sprintf
                  |L29.144|
;;;4671                               }
;;;4672                           #endif
;;;4673                       }
;;;4674   
;;;4675                       pcWriteBuffer += strlen( pcWriteBuffer ); /*lint !e9016 Pointer arithmetic ok on char pointers especially as in this case where it best denotes the intent of the code. */
000090  4630              MOV      r0,r6
000092  f7fffffe          BL       strlen
000096  4406              ADD      r6,r6,r0
000098  1c64              ADDS     r4,r4,#1              ;4632
                  |L29.154|
00009a  4544              CMP      r4,r8                 ;4632
00009c  d3d2              BCC      |L29.68|
                  |L29.158|
;;;4676                   }
;;;4677               }
;;;4678               else
;;;4679               {
;;;4680                   mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;4681               }
;;;4682   
;;;4683               /* Free the array again.  NOTE!  If configSUPPORT_DYNAMIC_ALLOCATION
;;;4684                * is 0 then vPortFree() will be #defined to nothing. */
;;;4685               vPortFree( pxTaskStatusArray );
00009e  4628              MOV      r0,r5
0000a0  f7fffffe          BL       vPortFree
                  |L29.164|
;;;4686           }
;;;4687           else
;;;4688           {
;;;4689               mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;4690           }
;;;4691       }
0000a4  e8bd83f8          POP      {r3-r9,pc}
;;;4692   
                          ENDP

                  |L29.168|
                          DCD      uxCurrentNumberOfTasks
                  |L29.172|
0000ac  09257509          DCB      "\t%u\t\t%u%%\r\n",0
0000b0  09257525
0000b4  250d0a00
                  |L29.184|
0000b8  09257509          DCB      "\t%u\t\t<1%%\r\n",0
0000bc  093c3125
0000c0  250d0a00

                          AREA ||i.vTaskInternalSetTimeOutState||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  vTaskInternalSetTimeOutState PROC
;;;3338   
;;;3339   void vTaskInternalSetTimeOutState( TimeOut_t * const pxTimeOut )
000000  4903              LDR      r1,|L30.16|
;;;3340   {
;;;3341       /* For internal use only as it does not use a critical section. */
;;;3342       pxTimeOut->xOverflowCount = xNumOfOverflows;
000002  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; xNumOfOverflows
000004  6001              STR      r1,[r0,#0]
;;;3343       pxTimeOut->xTimeOnEntering = xTickCount;
000006  4903              LDR      r1,|L30.20|
000008  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; xTickCount
00000a  6041              STR      r1,[r0,#4]
;;;3344   }
00000c  4770              BX       lr
;;;3345   /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
                          ENDP

00000e  0000              DCW      0x0000
                  |L30.16|
                          DCD      xNumOfOverflows
                  |L30.20|
                          DCD      xTickCount

                          AREA ||i.vTaskIsRunning||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  vTaskIsRunning PROC
;;;5441   
;;;5442   uint8_t vTaskIsRunning(void)
000000  4803              LDR      r0,|L31.16|
;;;5443   {
;;;5444       return (xSchedulerRunning == pdFALSE)?0:1;
000002  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; xSchedulerRunning
000004  b108              CBZ      r0,|L31.10|
000006  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
                  |L31.8|
;;;5445   }
000008  4770              BX       lr
                  |L31.10|
00000a  2000              MOVS     r0,#0                 ;5444
00000c  e7fc              B        |L31.8|
;;;5446   
                          ENDP

00000e  0000              DCW      0x0000
                  |L31.16|
                          DCD      xSchedulerRunning

                          AREA ||i.vTaskList||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                          REQUIRE _printf_percent
                          REQUIRE _printf_u
                          REQUIRE _printf_c
                          REQUIRE _printf_int_dec
                          REQUIRE _printf_str
                  vTaskList PROC
;;;4466   
;;;4467       void vTaskList( char * pcWriteBuffer )
000000  e92d41fc          PUSH     {r2-r8,lr}
;;;4468       {
000004  4606              MOV      r6,r0
;;;4469           TaskStatus_t * pxTaskStatusArray;
;;;4470           UBaseType_t uxArraySize, x;
;;;4471           char cStatus;
;;;4472   
;;;4473           /*
;;;4474            * PLEASE NOTE:
;;;4475            *
;;;4476            * This function is provided for convenience only, and is used by many
;;;4477            * of the demo applications.  Do not consider it to be part of the
;;;4478            * scheduler.
;;;4479            *
;;;4480            * vTaskList() calls uxTaskGetSystemState(), then formats part of the
;;;4481            * uxTaskGetSystemState() output into a human readable table that
;;;4482            * displays task names, states and stack usage.
;;;4483            *
;;;4484            * vTaskList() has a dependency on the sprintf() C library function that
;;;4485            * might bloat the code size, use a lot of stack, and provide different
;;;4486            * results on different platforms.  An alternative, tiny, third party,
;;;4487            * and limited functionality implementation of sprintf() is provided in
;;;4488            * many of the FreeRTOS/Demo sub-directories in a file called
;;;4489            * printf-stdarg.c (note printf-stdarg.c does not provide a full
;;;4490            * snprintf() implementation!).
;;;4491            *
;;;4492            * It is recommended that production systems call uxTaskGetSystemState()
;;;4493            * directly to get access to raw stats data, rather than indirectly
;;;4494            * through a call to vTaskList().
;;;4495            */
;;;4496   
;;;4497   
;;;4498           /* Make sure the write buffer does not contain a string. */
;;;4499           *pcWriteBuffer = ( char ) 0x00;
000006  2000              MOVS     r0,#0
000008  7030              STRB     r0,[r6,#0]
;;;4500   
;;;4501           /* Take a snapshot of the number of tasks in case it changes while this
;;;4502            * function is executing. */
;;;4503           uxArraySize = uxCurrentNumberOfTasks;
00000a  482f              LDR      r0,|L32.200|
00000c  6807              LDR      r7,[r0,#0]  ; uxCurrentNumberOfTasks
;;;4504   
;;;4505           /* Allocate an array index for each task.  NOTE!  if
;;;4506            * configSUPPORT_DYNAMIC_ALLOCATION is set to 0 then pvPortMalloc() will
;;;4507            * equate to NULL. */
;;;4508           pxTaskStatusArray = pvPortMalloc( uxCurrentNumberOfTasks * sizeof( TaskStatus_t ) ); /*lint !e9079 All values returned by pvPortMalloc() have at least the alignment required by the MCU's stack and this allocation allocates a struct that has the alignment requirements of a pointer. */
00000e  4601              MOV      r1,r0
000010  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; uxCurrentNumberOfTasks
000012  eb0101c1          ADD      r1,r1,r1,LSL #3
000016  0088              LSLS     r0,r1,#2
000018  f7fffffe          BL       pvPortMalloc
00001c  4605              MOV      r5,r0
;;;4509   
;;;4510           if( pxTaskStatusArray != NULL )
00001e  2d00              CMP      r5,#0
000020  d050              BEQ      |L32.196|
;;;4511           {
;;;4512               /* Generate the (binary) data. */
;;;4513               uxArraySize = uxTaskGetSystemState( pxTaskStatusArray, uxArraySize, NULL );
000022  2200              MOVS     r2,#0
000024  4639              MOV      r1,r7
000026  4628              MOV      r0,r5
000028  f7fffffe          BL       uxTaskGetSystemState
00002c  4607              MOV      r7,r0
;;;4514   
;;;4515               /* Create a human readable table from the binary data. */
;;;4516               for( x = 0; x < uxArraySize; x++ )
00002e  2400              MOVS     r4,#0
000030  e043              B        |L32.186|
                  |L32.50|
;;;4517               {
;;;4518                   switch( pxTaskStatusArray[ x ].eCurrentState )
000032  eb0400c4          ADD      r0,r4,r4,LSL #3
000036  eb050080          ADD      r0,r5,r0,LSL #2
00003a  7b00              LDRB     r0,[r0,#0xc]
00003c  2806              CMP      r0,#6
00003e  d213              BCS      |L32.104|
000040  e8dff000          TBB      [pc,r0]
000044  0306090c          DCB      0x03,0x06,0x09,0x0c
000048  0f13              DCB      0x0f,0x13
;;;4519                   {
;;;4520                       case eRunning:
;;;4521                           cStatus = tskRUNNING_CHAR;
00004a  f04f0858          MOV      r8,#0x58
;;;4522                           break;
00004e  e00f              B        |L32.112|
;;;4523   
;;;4524                       case eReady:
;;;4525                           cStatus = tskREADY_CHAR;
000050  f04f0852          MOV      r8,#0x52
;;;4526                           break;
000054  e00c              B        |L32.112|
;;;4527   
;;;4528                       case eBlocked:
;;;4529                           cStatus = tskBLOCKED_CHAR;
000056  f04f0842          MOV      r8,#0x42
;;;4530                           break;
00005a  e009              B        |L32.112|
;;;4531   
;;;4532                       case eSuspended:
;;;4533                           cStatus = tskSUSPENDED_CHAR;
00005c  f04f0853          MOV      r8,#0x53
;;;4534                           break;
000060  e006              B        |L32.112|
;;;4535   
;;;4536                       case eDeleted:
;;;4537                           cStatus = tskDELETED_CHAR;
000062  f04f0844          MOV      r8,#0x44
;;;4538                           break;
000066  e003              B        |L32.112|
                  |L32.104|
;;;4539   
;;;4540                       case eInvalid: /* Fall through. */
000068  bf00              NOP      
;;;4541                       default:       /* Should not get here, but it is included
;;;4542                                       * to prevent static checking errors. */
;;;4543                           cStatus = ( char ) 0x00;
00006a  f04f0800          MOV      r8,#0
;;;4544                           break;
00006e  bf00              NOP      
                  |L32.112|
000070  bf00              NOP                            ;4522
;;;4545                   }
;;;4546   
;;;4547                   /* Write the task name to the string, padding with spaces so it
;;;4548                    * can be printed in tabular form more easily. */
;;;4549                   pcWriteBuffer = prvWriteNameToBuffer( pcWriteBuffer, pxTaskStatusArray[ x ].pcTaskName );
000072  eb0400c4          ADD      r0,r4,r4,LSL #3
000076  eb050080          ADD      r0,r5,r0,LSL #2
00007a  6841              LDR      r1,[r0,#4]
00007c  4630              MOV      r0,r6
00007e  f7fffffe          BL       prvWriteNameToBuffer
000082  4606              MOV      r6,r0
;;;4550   
;;;4551                   /* Write the rest of the string. */
;;;4552                   sprintf( pcWriteBuffer, "\t%c\t%u\t%u\t%u\r\n", cStatus, ( unsigned int ) pxTaskStatusArray[ x ].uxCurrentPriority, ( unsigned int ) pxTaskStatusArray[ x ].usStackHighWaterMark, ( unsigned int ) pxTaskStatusArray[ x ].xTaskNumber ); /*lint !e586 sprintf() allowed as this is compiled with many compilers and this is a utility function only - not part of the core kernel implementation. */
000084  eb0400c4          ADD      r0,r4,r4,LSL #3
000088  eb050080          ADD      r0,r5,r0,LSL #2
00008c  6880              LDR      r0,[r0,#8]
00008e  eb0401c4          ADD      r1,r4,r4,LSL #3
000092  eb050181          ADD      r1,r5,r1,LSL #2
000096  8c09              LDRH     r1,[r1,#0x20]
000098  e9cd1000          STRD     r1,r0,[sp,#0]
00009c  eb0400c4          ADD      r0,r4,r4,LSL #3
0000a0  eb050080          ADD      r0,r5,r0,LSL #2
0000a4  4642              MOV      r2,r8
0000a6  a109              ADR      r1,|L32.204|
0000a8  6903              LDR      r3,[r0,#0x10]
0000aa  4630              MOV      r0,r6
0000ac  f7fffffe          BL       __2sprintf
;;;4553                   pcWriteBuffer += strlen( pcWriteBuffer );                                                                                                                                                                                                /*lint !e9016 Pointer arithmetic ok on char pointers especially as in this case where it best denotes the intent of the code. */
0000b0  4630              MOV      r0,r6
0000b2  f7fffffe          BL       strlen
0000b6  4406              ADD      r6,r6,r0
0000b8  1c64              ADDS     r4,r4,#1              ;4516
                  |L32.186|
0000ba  42bc              CMP      r4,r7                 ;4516
0000bc  d3b9              BCC      |L32.50|
;;;4554               }
;;;4555   
;;;4556               /* Free the array again.  NOTE!  If configSUPPORT_DYNAMIC_ALLOCATION
;;;4557                * is 0 then vPortFree() will be #defined to nothing. */
;;;4558               vPortFree( pxTaskStatusArray );
0000be  4628              MOV      r0,r5
0000c0  f7fffffe          BL       vPortFree
                  |L32.196|
;;;4559           }
;;;4560           else
;;;4561           {
;;;4562               mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;4563           }
;;;4564       }
0000c4  e8bd81fc          POP      {r2-r8,pc}
;;;4565   
                          ENDP

                  |L32.200|
                          DCD      uxCurrentNumberOfTasks
                  |L32.204|
0000cc  09256309          DCB      "\t%c\t%u\t%u\t%u\r\n",0
0000d0  25750925
0000d4  75092575
0000d8  0d0a00  
0000db  00                DCB      0

                          AREA ||i.vTaskMissedYield||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  vTaskMissedYield PROC
;;;3411   
;;;3412   void vTaskMissedYield( void )
000000  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
;;;3413   {
;;;3414       xYieldPending = pdTRUE;
000002  4901              LDR      r1,|L33.8|
000004  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; xYieldPending
;;;3415   }

;;;3416   /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
                          ENDP

                          AREA ||i.vTaskPlaceOnEventList||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  vTaskPlaceOnEventList PROC
;;;3130   
;;;3131   void vTaskPlaceOnEventList( List_t * const pxEventList,
000000  b570              PUSH     {r4-r6,lr}
;;;3132                               const TickType_t xTicksToWait )
;;;3133   {
000002  4605              MOV      r5,r0
000004  460c              MOV      r4,r1
;;;3134       configASSERT( pxEventList );
;;;3135   
;;;3136       /* THIS FUNCTION MUST BE CALLED WITH EITHER INTERRUPTS DISABLED OR THE
;;;3137        * SCHEDULER SUSPENDED AND THE QUEUE BEING ACCESSED LOCKED. */
;;;3138   
;;;3139       /* Place the event list item of the TCB in the appropriate event list.
;;;3140        * This is placed in the list in priority order so the highest priority task
;;;3141        * is the first to be woken by the event.  The queue that contains the event
;;;3142        * list is locked, preventing simultaneous access from interrupts. */
;;;3143       vListInsert( pxEventList, &( pxCurrentTCB->xEventListItem ) );
000006  4805              LDR      r0,|L34.28|
000008  6801              LDR      r1,[r0,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
00000a  3118              ADDS     r1,r1,#0x18
00000c  4628              MOV      r0,r5
00000e  f7fffffe          BL       vListInsert
;;;3144   
;;;3145       prvAddCurrentTaskToDelayedList( xTicksToWait, pdTRUE );
000012  2101              MOVS     r1,#1
000014  4620              MOV      r0,r4
000016  f7fffffe          BL       prvAddCurrentTaskToDelayedList
;;;3146   }
00001a  bd70              POP      {r4-r6,pc}
;;;3147   /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
                          ENDP

                  |L34.28|
                          DCD      pxCurrentTCB

                          AREA ||i.vTaskPlaceOnUnorderedEventList||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  vTaskPlaceOnUnorderedEventList PROC
;;;3148   
;;;3149   void vTaskPlaceOnUnorderedEventList( List_t * pxEventList,
000000  b570              PUSH     {r4-r6,lr}
;;;3150                                        const TickType_t xItemValue,
;;;3151                                        const TickType_t xTicksToWait )
;;;3152   {
000002  4606              MOV      r6,r0
000004  460c              MOV      r4,r1
000006  4615              MOV      r5,r2
;;;3153       configASSERT( pxEventList );
;;;3154   
;;;3155       /* THIS FUNCTION MUST BE CALLED WITH THE SCHEDULER SUSPENDED.  It is used by
;;;3156        * the event groups implementation. */
;;;3157       configASSERT( uxSchedulerSuspended != 0 );
;;;3158   
;;;3159       /* Store the item value in the event list item.  It is safe to access the
;;;3160        * event list item here as interrupts won't access the event list item of a
;;;3161        * task that is not in the Blocked state. */
;;;3162       listSET_LIST_ITEM_VALUE( &( pxCurrentTCB->xEventListItem ), xItemValue | taskEVENT_LIST_ITEM_VALUE_IN_USE );
000008  f0444000          ORR      r0,r4,#0x80000000
00000c  4906              LDR      r1,|L35.40|
00000e  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000010  6188              STR      r0,[r1,#0x18]
;;;3163   
;;;3164       /* Place the event list item of the TCB at the end of the appropriate event
;;;3165        * list.  It is safe to access the event list here because it is part of an
;;;3166        * event group implementation - and interrupts don't access event groups
;;;3167        * directly (instead they access them indirectly by pending function calls to
;;;3168        * the task level). */
;;;3169       vListInsertEnd( pxEventList, &( pxCurrentTCB->xEventListItem ) );
000012  4805              LDR      r0,|L35.40|
000014  6801              LDR      r1,[r0,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000016  3118              ADDS     r1,r1,#0x18
000018  4630              MOV      r0,r6
00001a  f7fffffe          BL       vListInsertEnd
;;;3170   
;;;3171       prvAddCurrentTaskToDelayedList( xTicksToWait, pdTRUE );
00001e  2101              MOVS     r1,#1
000020  4628              MOV      r0,r5
000022  f7fffffe          BL       prvAddCurrentTaskToDelayedList
;;;3172   }
000026  bd70              POP      {r4-r6,pc}
;;;3173   /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
                          ENDP

                  |L35.40|
                          DCD      pxCurrentTCB

                          AREA ||i.vTaskPriorityDisinheritAfterTimeout||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  vTaskPriorityDisinheritAfterTimeout PROC
;;;4270   
;;;4271       void vTaskPriorityDisinheritAfterTimeout( TaskHandle_t const pxMutexHolder,
000000  e92d47f0          PUSH     {r4-r10,lr}
;;;4272                                                 UBaseType_t uxHighestPriorityWaitingTask )
;;;4273       {
000004  4606              MOV      r6,r0
000006  460f              MOV      r7,r1
;;;4274           TCB_t * const pxTCB = pxMutexHolder;
000008  4634              MOV      r4,r6
;;;4275           UBaseType_t uxPriorityUsedOnEntry, uxPriorityToUse;
;;;4276           const UBaseType_t uxOnlyOneMutexHeld = ( UBaseType_t ) 1;
00000a  f04f0901          MOV      r9,#1
;;;4277   
;;;4278           if( pxMutexHolder != NULL )
00000e  2e00              CMP      r6,#0
000010  d040              BEQ      |L36.148|
;;;4279           {
;;;4280               /* If pxMutexHolder is not NULL then the holder must hold at least
;;;4281                * one mutex. */
;;;4282               configASSERT( pxTCB->uxMutexesHeld );
;;;4283   
;;;4284               /* Determine the priority to which the priority of the task that
;;;4285                * holds the mutex should be set.  This will be the greater of the
;;;4286                * holding task's base priority and the priority of the highest
;;;4287                * priority task that is waiting to obtain the mutex. */
;;;4288               if( pxTCB->uxBasePriority < uxHighestPriorityWaitingTask )
000012  6ce0              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x4c]
000014  42b8              CMP      r0,r7
000016  d201              BCS      |L36.28|
;;;4289               {
;;;4290                   uxPriorityToUse = uxHighestPriorityWaitingTask;
000018  463d              MOV      r5,r7
00001a  e000              B        |L36.30|
                  |L36.28|
;;;4291               }
;;;4292               else
;;;4293               {
;;;4294                   uxPriorityToUse = pxTCB->uxBasePriority;
00001c  6ce5              LDR      r5,[r4,#0x4c]
                  |L36.30|
;;;4295               }
;;;4296   
;;;4297               /* Does the priority need to change? */
;;;4298               if( pxTCB->uxPriority != uxPriorityToUse )
00001e  6ae0              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x2c]
000020  42a8              CMP      r0,r5
000022  d037              BEQ      |L36.148|
;;;4299               {
;;;4300                   /* Only disinherit if no other mutexes are held.  This is a
;;;4301                    * simplification in the priority inheritance implementation.  If
;;;4302                    * the task that holds the mutex is also holding other mutexes then
;;;4303                    * the other mutexes may have caused the priority inheritance. */
;;;4304                   if( pxTCB->uxMutexesHeld == uxOnlyOneMutexHeld )
000024  6d20              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x50]
000026  2801              CMP      r0,#1
000028  d134              BNE      |L36.148|
;;;4305                   {
;;;4306                       /* If a task has timed out because it already holds the
;;;4307                        * mutex it was trying to obtain then it cannot of inherited
;;;4308                        * its own priority. */
;;;4309                       configASSERT( pxTCB != pxCurrentTCB );
;;;4310   
;;;4311                       /* Disinherit the priority, remembering the previous
;;;4312                        * priority to facilitate determining the subject task's
;;;4313                        * state. */
;;;4314                       traceTASK_PRIORITY_DISINHERIT( pxTCB, uxPriorityToUse );
;;;4315                       uxPriorityUsedOnEntry = pxTCB->uxPriority;
00002a  f8d4802c          LDR      r8,[r4,#0x2c]
;;;4316                       pxTCB->uxPriority = uxPriorityToUse;
00002e  62e5              STR      r5,[r4,#0x2c]
;;;4317   
;;;4318                       /* Only reset the event list item value if the value is not
;;;4319                        * being used for anything else. */
;;;4320                       if( ( listGET_LIST_ITEM_VALUE( &( pxTCB->xEventListItem ) ) & taskEVENT_LIST_ITEM_VALUE_IN_USE ) == 0UL )
000030  69a0              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x18]
000032  f0004000          AND      r0,r0,#0x80000000
000036  b910              CBNZ     r0,|L36.62|
;;;4321                       {
;;;4322                           listSET_LIST_ITEM_VALUE( &( pxTCB->xEventListItem ), ( TickType_t ) configMAX_PRIORITIES - ( TickType_t ) uxPriorityToUse ); /*lint !e961 MISRA exception as the casts are only redundant for some ports. */
000038  f1c5000f          RSB      r0,r5,#0xf
00003c  61a0              STR      r0,[r4,#0x18]
                  |L36.62|
;;;4323                       }
;;;4324                       else
;;;4325                       {
;;;4326                           mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;4327                       }
;;;4328   
;;;4329                       /* If the running task is not the task that holds the mutex
;;;4330                        * then the task that holds the mutex could be in either the
;;;4331                        * Ready, Blocked or Suspended states.  Only remove the task
;;;4332                        * from its current state list if it is in the Ready state as
;;;4333                        * the task's priority is going to change and there is one
;;;4334                        * Ready list per priority. */
;;;4335                       if( listIS_CONTAINED_WITHIN( &( pxReadyTasksLists[ uxPriorityUsedOnEntry ] ), &( pxTCB->xStateListItem ) ) != pdFALSE )
00003e  eb080188          ADD      r1,r8,r8,LSL #2
000042  4a15              LDR      r2,|L36.152|
000044  eb020181          ADD      r1,r2,r1,LSL #2
000048  6960              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x14]
00004a  4288              CMP      r0,r1
00004c  d101              BNE      |L36.82|
00004e  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
000050  e000              B        |L36.84|
                  |L36.82|
000052  2000              MOVS     r0,#0
                  |L36.84|
000054  b1f0              CBZ      r0,|L36.148|
;;;4336                       {
;;;4337                           if( uxListRemove( &( pxTCB->xStateListItem ) ) == ( UBaseType_t ) 0 )
000056  1d20              ADDS     r0,r4,#4
000058  f7fffffe          BL       uxListRemove
00005c  b940              CBNZ     r0,|L36.112|
;;;4338                           {
