;;;4339                               /* It is known that the task is in its ready list so
;;;4340                                * there is no need to check again and the port level
;;;4341                                * reset macro can be called directly. */
;;;4342                               portRESET_READY_PRIORITY( pxTCB->uxPriority, uxTopReadyPriority );
00005e  f894102c          LDRB     r1,[r4,#0x2c]
000062  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
000064  4088              LSLS     r0,r0,r1
000066  490d              LDR      r1,|L36.156|
000068  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
00006a  4381              BICS     r1,r1,r0
00006c  480b              LDR      r0,|L36.156|
00006e  6001              STR      r1,[r0,#0]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
                  |L36.112|
;;;4343                           }
;;;4344                           else
;;;4345                           {
;;;4346                               mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;4347                           }
;;;4348   
;;;4349                           prvAddTaskToReadyList( pxTCB );
000070  f894102c          LDRB     r1,[r4,#0x2c]
000074  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
000076  4088              LSLS     r0,r0,r1
000078  4908              LDR      r1,|L36.156|
00007a  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
00007c  4308              ORRS     r0,r0,r1
00007e  4907              LDR      r1,|L36.156|
000080  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
000082  6ae1              LDR      r1,[r4,#0x2c]
000084  eb010181          ADD      r1,r1,r1,LSL #2
000088  4a03              LDR      r2,|L36.152|
00008a  eb020081          ADD      r0,r2,r1,LSL #2
00008e  1d21              ADDS     r1,r4,#4
000090  f7fffffe          BL       vListInsertEnd
                  |L36.148|
;;;4350                       }
;;;4351                       else
;;;4352                       {
;;;4353                           mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;4354                       }
;;;4355                   }
;;;4356                   else
;;;4357                   {
;;;4358                       mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;4359                   }
;;;4360               }
;;;4361               else
;;;4362               {
;;;4363                   mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;4364               }
;;;4365           }
;;;4366           else
;;;4367           {
;;;4368               mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;4369           }
;;;4370       }
000094  e8bd87f0          POP      {r4-r10,pc}
;;;4371   
                          ENDP

                  |L36.152|
                          DCD      pxReadyTasksLists
                  |L36.156|
                          DCD      uxTopReadyPriority

                          AREA ||i.vTaskPrioritySet||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  vTaskPrioritySet PROC
;;;1584   
;;;1585       void vTaskPrioritySet( TaskHandle_t xTask,
000000  e92d47f0          PUSH     {r4-r10,lr}
;;;1586                              UBaseType_t uxNewPriority )
;;;1587       {
000004  4680              MOV      r8,r0
000006  460d              MOV      r5,r1
;;;1588           TCB_t * pxTCB;
;;;1589           UBaseType_t uxCurrentBasePriority, uxPriorityUsedOnEntry;
;;;1590           BaseType_t xYieldRequired = pdFALSE;
000008  f04f0900          MOV      r9,#0
;;;1591   
;;;1592           configASSERT( ( uxNewPriority < configMAX_PRIORITIES ) );
;;;1593   
;;;1594           /* Ensure the new priority is valid. */
;;;1595           if( uxNewPriority >= ( UBaseType_t ) configMAX_PRIORITIES )
00000c  2d0f              CMP      r5,#0xf
00000e  d300              BCC      |L37.18|
;;;1596           {
;;;1597               uxNewPriority = ( UBaseType_t ) configMAX_PRIORITIES - ( UBaseType_t ) 1U;
000010  250e              MOVS     r5,#0xe
                  |L37.18|
;;;1598           }
;;;1599           else
;;;1600           {
;;;1601               mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1602           }
;;;1603   
;;;1604           taskENTER_CRITICAL();
000012  f7fffffe          BL       vPortEnterCritical
;;;1605           {
;;;1606               /* If null is passed in here then it is the priority of the calling
;;;1607                * task that is being changed. */
;;;1608               pxTCB = prvGetTCBFromHandle( xTask );
000016  f1b80f00          CMP      r8,#0
00001a  d102              BNE      |L37.34|
00001c  4831              LDR      r0,|L37.228|
00001e  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000020  e000              B        |L37.36|
                  |L37.34|
000022  4640              MOV      r0,r8
                  |L37.36|
000024  4604              MOV      r4,r0
;;;1609   
;;;1610               traceTASK_PRIORITY_SET( pxTCB, uxNewPriority );
;;;1611   
;;;1612               #if ( configUSE_MUTEXES == 1 )
;;;1613                   {
;;;1614                       uxCurrentBasePriority = pxTCB->uxBasePriority;
000026  6ce6              LDR      r6,[r4,#0x4c]
;;;1615                   }
;;;1616               #else
;;;1617                   {
;;;1618                       uxCurrentBasePriority = pxTCB->uxPriority;
;;;1619                   }
;;;1620               #endif
;;;1621   
;;;1622               if( uxCurrentBasePriority != uxNewPriority )
000028  42ae              CMP      r6,r5
00002a  d056              BEQ      |L37.218|
;;;1623               {
;;;1624                   /* The priority change may have readied a task of higher
;;;1625                    * priority than the calling task. */
;;;1626                   if( uxNewPriority > uxCurrentBasePriority )
00002c  42b5              CMP      r5,r6
00002e  d90b              BLS      |L37.72|
;;;1627                   {
;;;1628                       if( pxTCB != pxCurrentTCB )
000030  482c              LDR      r0,|L37.228|
000032  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000034  4284              CMP      r4,r0
000036  d00d              BEQ      |L37.84|
;;;1629                       {
;;;1630                           /* The priority of a task other than the currently
;;;1631                            * running task is being raised.  Is the priority being
;;;1632                            * raised above that of the running task? */
;;;1633                           if( uxNewPriority >= pxCurrentTCB->uxPriority )
000038  482a              LDR      r0,|L37.228|
00003a  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
00003c  6ac0              LDR      r0,[r0,#0x2c]
00003e  42a8              CMP      r0,r5
000040  d808              BHI      |L37.84|
;;;1634                           {
;;;1635                               xYieldRequired = pdTRUE;
000042  f04f0901          MOV      r9,#1
000046  e005              B        |L37.84|
                  |L37.72|
;;;1636                           }
;;;1637                           else
;;;1638                           {
;;;1639                               mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1640                           }
;;;1641                       }
;;;1642                       else
;;;1643                       {
;;;1644                           /* The priority of the running task is being raised,
;;;1645                            * but the running task must already be the highest
;;;1646                            * priority task able to run so no yield is required. */
;;;1647                       }
;;;1648                   }
;;;1649                   else if( pxTCB == pxCurrentTCB )
000048  4826              LDR      r0,|L37.228|
00004a  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
00004c  4284              CMP      r4,r0
00004e  d101              BNE      |L37.84|
;;;1650                   {
;;;1651                       /* Setting the priority of the running task down means
;;;1652                        * there may now be another task of higher priority that
;;;1653                        * is ready to execute. */
;;;1654                       xYieldRequired = pdTRUE;
000050  f04f0901          MOV      r9,#1
                  |L37.84|
;;;1655                   }
;;;1656                   else
;;;1657                   {
;;;1658                       /* Setting the priority of any other task down does not
;;;1659                        * require a yield as the running task must be above the
;;;1660                        * new priority of the task being modified. */
;;;1661                   }
;;;1662   
;;;1663                   /* Remember the ready list the task might be referenced from
;;;1664                    * before its uxPriority member is changed so the
;;;1665                    * taskRESET_READY_PRIORITY() macro can function correctly. */
;;;1666                   uxPriorityUsedOnEntry = pxTCB->uxPriority;
000054  6ae7              LDR      r7,[r4,#0x2c]
;;;1667   
;;;1668                   #if ( configUSE_MUTEXES == 1 )
;;;1669                       {
;;;1670                           /* Only change the priority being used if the task is not
;;;1671                            * currently using an inherited priority. */
;;;1672                           if( pxTCB->uxBasePriority == pxTCB->uxPriority )
000056  6ae1              LDR      r1,[r4,#0x2c]
000058  6ce0              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x4c]
00005a  4288              CMP      r0,r1
00005c  d100              BNE      |L37.96|
;;;1673                           {
;;;1674                               pxTCB->uxPriority = uxNewPriority;
00005e  62e5              STR      r5,[r4,#0x2c]
                  |L37.96|
;;;1675                           }
;;;1676                           else
;;;1677                           {
;;;1678                               mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1679                           }
;;;1680   
;;;1681                           /* The base priority gets set whatever. */
;;;1682                           pxTCB->uxBasePriority = uxNewPriority;
000060  64e5              STR      r5,[r4,#0x4c]
;;;1683                       }
;;;1684                   #else /* if ( configUSE_MUTEXES == 1 ) */
;;;1685                       {
;;;1686                           pxTCB->uxPriority = uxNewPriority;
;;;1687                       }
;;;1688                   #endif /* if ( configUSE_MUTEXES == 1 ) */
;;;1689   
;;;1690                   /* Only reset the event list item value if the value is not
;;;1691                    * being used for anything else. */
;;;1692                   if( ( listGET_LIST_ITEM_VALUE( &( pxTCB->xEventListItem ) ) & taskEVENT_LIST_ITEM_VALUE_IN_USE ) == 0UL )
000062  69a0              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x18]
000064  f0004000          AND      r0,r0,#0x80000000
000068  b910              CBNZ     r0,|L37.112|
;;;1693                   {
;;;1694                       listSET_LIST_ITEM_VALUE( &( pxTCB->xEventListItem ), ( ( TickType_t ) configMAX_PRIORITIES - ( TickType_t ) uxNewPriority ) ); /*lint !e961 MISRA exception as the casts are only redundant for some ports. */
00006a  f1c5000f          RSB      r0,r5,#0xf
00006e  61a0              STR      r0,[r4,#0x18]
                  |L37.112|
;;;1695                   }
;;;1696                   else
;;;1697                   {
;;;1698                       mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1699                   }
;;;1700   
;;;1701                   /* If the task is in the blocked or suspended list we need do
;;;1702                    * nothing more than change its priority variable. However, if
;;;1703                    * the task is in a ready list it needs to be removed and placed
;;;1704                    * in the list appropriate to its new priority. */
;;;1705                   if( listIS_CONTAINED_WITHIN( &( pxReadyTasksLists[ uxPriorityUsedOnEntry ] ), &( pxTCB->xStateListItem ) ) != pdFALSE )
000070  eb070187          ADD      r1,r7,r7,LSL #2
000074  4a1c              LDR      r2,|L37.232|
000076  eb020181          ADD      r1,r2,r1,LSL #2
00007a  6960              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x14]
00007c  4288              CMP      r0,r1
00007e  d101              BNE      |L37.132|
000080  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
000082  e000              B        |L37.134|
                  |L37.132|
000084  2000              MOVS     r0,#0
                  |L37.134|
000086  b1e0              CBZ      r0,|L37.194|
;;;1706                   {
;;;1707                       /* The task is currently in its ready list - remove before
;;;1708                        * adding it to it's new ready list.  As we are in a critical
;;;1709                        * section we can do this even if the scheduler is suspended. */
;;;1710                       if( uxListRemove( &( pxTCB->xStateListItem ) ) == ( UBaseType_t ) 0 )
000088  1d20              ADDS     r0,r4,#4
00008a  f7fffffe          BL       uxListRemove
00008e  b930              CBNZ     r0,|L37.158|
;;;1711                       {
;;;1712                           /* It is known that the task is in its ready list so
;;;1713                            * there is no need to check again and the port level
;;;1714                            * reset macro can be called directly. */
;;;1715                           portRESET_READY_PRIORITY( uxPriorityUsedOnEntry, uxTopReadyPriority );
000090  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
000092  40b8              LSLS     r0,r0,r7
000094  4915              LDR      r1,|L37.236|
000096  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
000098  4381              BICS     r1,r1,r0
00009a  4814              LDR      r0,|L37.236|
00009c  6001              STR      r1,[r0,#0]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
                  |L37.158|
;;;1716                       }
;;;1717                       else
;;;1718                       {
;;;1719                           mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1720                       }
;;;1721   
;;;1722                       prvAddTaskToReadyList( pxTCB );
00009e  f894102c          LDRB     r1,[r4,#0x2c]
0000a2  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
0000a4  4088              LSLS     r0,r0,r1
0000a6  4911              LDR      r1,|L37.236|
0000a8  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
0000aa  4308              ORRS     r0,r0,r1
0000ac  490f              LDR      r1,|L37.236|
0000ae  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
0000b0  6ae1              LDR      r1,[r4,#0x2c]
0000b2  eb010181          ADD      r1,r1,r1,LSL #2
0000b6  4a0c              LDR      r2,|L37.232|
0000b8  eb020081          ADD      r0,r2,r1,LSL #2
0000bc  1d21              ADDS     r1,r4,#4
0000be  f7fffffe          BL       vListInsertEnd
                  |L37.194|
;;;1723                   }
;;;1724                   else
;;;1725                   {
;;;1726                       mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1727                   }
;;;1728   
;;;1729                   if( xYieldRequired != pdFALSE )
0000c2  f1b90f00          CMP      r9,#0
0000c6  d007              BEQ      |L37.216|
;;;1730                   {
;;;1731                       taskYIELD_IF_USING_PREEMPTION();
0000c8  f04f5080          MOV      r0,#0x10000000
0000cc  4908              LDR      r1,|L37.240|
0000ce  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]
0000d0  f3bf8f4f          DSB      
0000d4  f3bf8f6f          ISB      
                  |L37.216|
;;;1732                   }
;;;1733                   else
;;;1734                   {
;;;1735                       mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1736                   }
;;;1737   
;;;1738                   /* Remove compiler warning about unused variables when the port
;;;1739                    * optimised task selection is not being used. */
;;;1740                   ( void ) uxPriorityUsedOnEntry;
0000d8  bf00              NOP      
                  |L37.218|
;;;1741               }
;;;1742           }
;;;1743           taskEXIT_CRITICAL();
0000da  f7fffffe          BL       vPortExitCritical
;;;1744       }
0000de  e8bd87f0          POP      {r4-r10,pc}
;;;1745   
                          ENDP

0000e2  0000              DCW      0x0000
                  |L37.228|
                          DCD      pxCurrentTCB
                  |L37.232|
                          DCD      pxReadyTasksLists
                  |L37.236|
                          DCD      uxTopReadyPriority
                  |L37.240|
                          DCD      0xe000ed04

                          AREA ||i.vTaskRemoveFromUnorderedEventList||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  vTaskRemoveFromUnorderedEventList PROC
;;;3277   
;;;3278   void vTaskRemoveFromUnorderedEventList( ListItem_t * pxEventListItem,
000000  b570              PUSH     {r4-r6,lr}
;;;3279                                           const TickType_t xItemValue )
;;;3280   {
000002  4605              MOV      r5,r0
000004  460e              MOV      r6,r1
;;;3281       TCB_t * pxUnblockedTCB;
;;;3282   
;;;3283       /* THIS FUNCTION MUST BE CALLED WITH THE SCHEDULER SUSPENDED.  It is used by
;;;3284        * the event flags implementation. */
;;;3285       configASSERT( uxSchedulerSuspended != pdFALSE );
;;;3286   
;;;3287       /* Store the new item value in the event list. */
;;;3288       listSET_LIST_ITEM_VALUE( pxEventListItem, xItemValue | taskEVENT_LIST_ITEM_VALUE_IN_USE );
000006  f0464000          ORR      r0,r6,#0x80000000
00000a  6028              STR      r0,[r5,#0]
;;;3289   
;;;3290       /* Remove the event list form the event flag.  Interrupts do not access
;;;3291        * event flags. */
;;;3292       pxUnblockedTCB = listGET_LIST_ITEM_OWNER( pxEventListItem ); /*lint !e9079 void * is used as this macro is used with timers and co-routines too.  Alignment is known to be fine as the type of the pointer stored and retrieved is the same. */
00000c  68ec              LDR      r4,[r5,#0xc]
;;;3293       configASSERT( pxUnblockedTCB );
;;;3294       ( void ) uxListRemove( pxEventListItem );
00000e  4628              MOV      r0,r5
000010  f7fffffe          BL       uxListRemove
;;;3295   
;;;3296       #if ( configUSE_TICKLESS_IDLE != 0 )
;;;3297           {
;;;3298               /* If a task is blocked on a kernel object then xNextTaskUnblockTime
;;;3299                * might be set to the blocked task's time out time.  If the task is
;;;3300                * unblocked for a reason other than a timeout xNextTaskUnblockTime is
;;;3301                * normally left unchanged, because it is automatically reset to a new
;;;3302                * value when the tick count equals xNextTaskUnblockTime.  However if
;;;3303                * tickless idling is used it might be more important to enter sleep mode
;;;3304                * at the earliest possible time - so reset xNextTaskUnblockTime here to
;;;3305                * ensure it is updated at the earliest possible time. */
;;;3306               prvResetNextTaskUnblockTime();
;;;3307           }
;;;3308       #endif
;;;3309   
;;;3310       /* Remove the task from the delayed list and add it to the ready list.  The
;;;3311        * scheduler is suspended so interrupts will not be accessing the ready
;;;3312        * lists. */
;;;3313       ( void ) uxListRemove( &( pxUnblockedTCB->xStateListItem ) );
000014  1d20              ADDS     r0,r4,#4
000016  f7fffffe          BL       uxListRemove
;;;3314       prvAddTaskToReadyList( pxUnblockedTCB );
00001a  f894102c          LDRB     r1,[r4,#0x2c]
00001e  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
000020  4088              LSLS     r0,r0,r1
000022  490c              LDR      r1,|L38.84|
000024  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
000026  4308              ORRS     r0,r0,r1
000028  490a              LDR      r1,|L38.84|
00002a  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
00002c  6ae1              LDR      r1,[r4,#0x2c]
00002e  eb010181          ADD      r1,r1,r1,LSL #2
000032  4a09              LDR      r2,|L38.88|
000034  eb020081          ADD      r0,r2,r1,LSL #2
000038  1d21              ADDS     r1,r4,#4
00003a  f7fffffe          BL       vListInsertEnd
;;;3315   
;;;3316       if( pxUnblockedTCB->uxPriority > pxCurrentTCB->uxPriority )
00003e  4907              LDR      r1,|L38.92|
000040  6ae0              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x2c]
000042  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000044  6ac9              LDR      r1,[r1,#0x2c]
000046  4288              CMP      r0,r1
000048  d902              BLS      |L38.80|
;;;3317       {
;;;3318           /* The unblocked task has a priority above that of the calling task, so
;;;3319            * a context switch is required.  This function is called with the
;;;3320            * scheduler suspended so xYieldPending is set so the context switch
;;;3321            * occurs immediately that the scheduler is resumed (unsuspended). */
;;;3322           xYieldPending = pdTRUE;
00004a  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
00004c  4904              LDR      r1,|L38.96|
00004e  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; xYieldPending
                  |L38.80|
;;;3323       }
;;;3324   }
000050  bd70              POP      {r4-r6,pc}
;;;3325   /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
                          ENDP

000052  0000              DCW      0x0000
                  |L38.84|
                          DCD      uxTopReadyPriority
                  |L38.88|
                          DCD      pxReadyTasksLists
                  |L38.92|
                          DCD      pxCurrentTCB
                  |L38.96|
                          DCD      xYieldPending

                          AREA ||i.vTaskResume||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  vTaskResume PROC
;;;1902   
;;;1903       void vTaskResume( TaskHandle_t xTaskToResume )
000000  b570              PUSH     {r4-r6,lr}
;;;1904       {
000002  4605              MOV      r5,r0
;;;1905           TCB_t * const pxTCB = xTaskToResume;
000004  462c              MOV      r4,r5
;;;1906   
;;;1907           /* It does not make sense to resume the calling task. */
;;;1908           configASSERT( xTaskToResume );
;;;1909   
;;;1910           /* The parameter cannot be NULL as it is impossible to resume the
;;;1911            * currently executing task. */
;;;1912           if( ( pxTCB != pxCurrentTCB ) && ( pxTCB != NULL ) )
000006  4818              LDR      r0,|L39.104|
000008  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
00000a  4284              CMP      r4,r0
00000c  d02b              BEQ      |L39.102|
00000e  b354              CBZ      r4,|L39.102|
;;;1913           {
;;;1914               taskENTER_CRITICAL();
000010  f7fffffe          BL       vPortEnterCritical
;;;1915               {
;;;1916                   if( prvTaskIsTaskSuspended( pxTCB ) != pdFALSE )
000014  4620              MOV      r0,r4
000016  f7fffffe          BL       prvTaskIsTaskSuspended
00001a  b310              CBZ      r0,|L39.98|
;;;1917                   {
;;;1918                       traceTASK_RESUME( pxTCB );
;;;1919   
;;;1920                       /* The ready list can be accessed even if the scheduler is
;;;1921                        * suspended because this is inside a critical section. */
;;;1922                       ( void ) uxListRemove( &( pxTCB->xStateListItem ) );
00001c  1d20              ADDS     r0,r4,#4
00001e  f7fffffe          BL       uxListRemove
;;;1923                       prvAddTaskToReadyList( pxTCB );
000022  f894102c          LDRB     r1,[r4,#0x2c]
000026  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
000028  4088              LSLS     r0,r0,r1
00002a  4910              LDR      r1,|L39.108|
00002c  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
00002e  4308              ORRS     r0,r0,r1
000030  490e              LDR      r1,|L39.108|
000032  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
000034  6ae1              LDR      r1,[r4,#0x2c]
000036  eb010181          ADD      r1,r1,r1,LSL #2
00003a  4a0d              LDR      r2,|L39.112|
00003c  eb020081          ADD      r0,r2,r1,LSL #2
000040  1d21              ADDS     r1,r4,#4
000042  f7fffffe          BL       vListInsertEnd
;;;1924   
;;;1925                       /* A higher priority task may have just been resumed. */
;;;1926                       if( pxTCB->uxPriority >= pxCurrentTCB->uxPriority )
000046  4908              LDR      r1,|L39.104|
000048  6ae0              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x2c]
00004a  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
00004c  6ac9              LDR      r1,[r1,#0x2c]
00004e  4288              CMP      r0,r1
000050  d307              BCC      |L39.98|
;;;1927                       {
;;;1928                           /* This yield may not cause the task just resumed to run,
;;;1929                            * but will leave the lists in the correct state for the
;;;1930                            * next yield. */
;;;1931                           taskYIELD_IF_USING_PREEMPTION();
000052  f04f5080          MOV      r0,#0x10000000
000056  4907              LDR      r1,|L39.116|
000058  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]
00005a  f3bf8f4f          DSB      
00005e  f3bf8f6f          ISB      
                  |L39.98|
;;;1932                       }
;;;1933                       else
;;;1934                       {
;;;1935                           mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1936                       }
;;;1937                   }
;;;1938                   else
;;;1939                   {
;;;1940                       mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1941                   }
;;;1942               }
;;;1943               taskEXIT_CRITICAL();
000062  f7fffffe          BL       vPortExitCritical
                  |L39.102|
;;;1944           }
;;;1945           else
;;;1946           {
;;;1947               mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1948           }
;;;1949       }
000066  bd70              POP      {r4-r6,pc}
;;;1950   
                          ENDP

                  |L39.104|
                          DCD      pxCurrentTCB
                  |L39.108|
                          DCD      uxTopReadyPriority
                  |L39.112|
                          DCD      pxReadyTasksLists
                  |L39.116|
                          DCD      0xe000ed04

                          AREA ||i.vTaskSetTaskNumber||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=1

                  vTaskSetTaskNumber PROC
;;;3442   
;;;3443       void vTaskSetTaskNumber( TaskHandle_t xTask,
000000  b108              CBZ      r0,|L40.6|
;;;3444                                const UBaseType_t uxHandle )
;;;3445       {
;;;3446           TCB_t * pxTCB;
;;;3447   
;;;3448           if( xTask != NULL )
;;;3449           {
;;;3450               pxTCB = xTask;
000002  4602              MOV      r2,r0
;;;3451               pxTCB->uxTaskNumber = uxHandle;
000004  6491              STR      r1,[r2,#0x48]
                  |L40.6|
;;;3452           }
;;;3453       }
000006  4770              BX       lr
;;;3454   
                          ENDP


                          AREA ||i.vTaskSetTimeOutState||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  vTaskSetTimeOutState PROC
;;;3326   
;;;3327   void vTaskSetTimeOutState( TimeOut_t * const pxTimeOut )
000000  b510              PUSH     {r4,lr}
;;;3328   {
000002  4604              MOV      r4,r0
;;;3329       configASSERT( pxTimeOut );
;;;3330       taskENTER_CRITICAL();
000004  f7fffffe          BL       vPortEnterCritical
;;;3331       {
;;;3332           pxTimeOut->xOverflowCount = xNumOfOverflows;
000008  4804              LDR      r0,|L41.28|
00000a  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; xNumOfOverflows
00000c  6020              STR      r0,[r4,#0]
;;;3333           pxTimeOut->xTimeOnEntering = xTickCount;
00000e  4804              LDR      r0,|L41.32|
000010  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; xTickCount
000012  6060              STR      r0,[r4,#4]
;;;3334       }
;;;3335       taskEXIT_CRITICAL();
000014  f7fffffe          BL       vPortExitCritical
;;;3336   }
000018  bd10              POP      {r4,pc}
;;;3337   /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
                          ENDP

00001a  0000              DCW      0x0000
                  |L41.28|
                          DCD      xNumOfOverflows
                  |L41.32|
                          DCD      xTickCount

                          AREA ||i.vTaskStartScheduler||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  vTaskStartScheduler PROC
;;;2031   
;;;2032   void vTaskStartScheduler( void )
000000  b51c              PUSH     {r2-r4,lr}
;;;2033   {
;;;2034       BaseType_t xReturn;
;;;2035   
;;;2036       /* Add the idle task at the lowest priority. */
;;;2037       #if ( configSUPPORT_STATIC_ALLOCATION == 1 )
;;;2038           {
;;;2039               StaticTask_t * pxIdleTaskTCBBuffer = NULL;
;;;2040               StackType_t * pxIdleTaskStackBuffer = NULL;
;;;2041               uint32_t ulIdleTaskStackSize;
;;;2042   
;;;2043               /* The Idle task is created using user provided RAM - obtain the
;;;2044                * address of the RAM then create the idle task. */
;;;2045               vApplicationGetIdleTaskMemory( &pxIdleTaskTCBBuffer, &pxIdleTaskStackBuffer, &ulIdleTaskStackSize );
;;;2046               xIdleTaskHandle = xTaskCreateStatic( prvIdleTask,
;;;2047                                                    configIDLE_TASK_NAME,
;;;2048                                                    ulIdleTaskStackSize,
;;;2049                                                    ( void * ) NULL,       /*lint !e961.  The cast is not redundant for all compilers. */
;;;2050                                                    portPRIVILEGE_BIT,     /* In effect ( tskIDLE_PRIORITY | portPRIVILEGE_BIT ), but tskIDLE_PRIORITY is zero. */
;;;2051                                                    pxIdleTaskStackBuffer,
;;;2052                                                    pxIdleTaskTCBBuffer ); /*lint !e961 MISRA exception, justified as it is not a redundant explicit cast to all supported compilers. */
;;;2053   
;;;2054               if( xIdleTaskHandle != NULL )
;;;2055               {
;;;2056                   xReturn = pdPASS;
;;;2057               }
;;;2058               else
;;;2059               {
;;;2060                   xReturn = pdFAIL;
;;;2061               }
;;;2062           }
;;;2063       #else /* if ( configSUPPORT_STATIC_ALLOCATION == 1 ) */
;;;2064           {
;;;2065               /* The Idle task is being created using dynamically allocated RAM. */
;;;2066               xReturn = xTaskCreate( prvIdleTask,
000002  4813              LDR      r0,|L42.80|
000004  2100              MOVS     r1,#0
000006  460b              MOV      r3,r1
000008  2280              MOVS     r2,#0x80
00000a  e9cd1000          STRD     r1,r0,[sp,#0]
00000e  a111              ADR      r1,|L42.84|
000010  4812              LDR      r0,|L42.92|
000012  f7fffffe          BL       xTaskCreate
000016  4604              MOV      r4,r0
;;;2067                                      configIDLE_TASK_NAME,
;;;2068                                      configMINIMAL_STACK_SIZE,
;;;2069                                      ( void * ) NULL,
;;;2070                                      portPRIVILEGE_BIT,  /* In effect ( tskIDLE_PRIORITY | portPRIVILEGE_BIT ), but tskIDLE_PRIORITY is zero. */
;;;2071                                      &xIdleTaskHandle ); /*lint !e961 MISRA exception, justified as it is not a redundant explicit cast to all supported compilers. */
;;;2072           }
;;;2073       #endif /* configSUPPORT_STATIC_ALLOCATION */
;;;2074   
;;;2075       #if ( configUSE_TIMERS == 1 )
;;;2076           {
;;;2077               if( xReturn == pdPASS )
;;;2078               {
;;;2079                   xReturn = xTimerCreateTimerTask();
;;;2080               }
;;;2081               else
;;;2082               {
;;;2083                   mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;2084               }
;;;2085           }
;;;2086       #endif /* configUSE_TIMERS */
;;;2087   
;;;2088       if( xReturn == pdPASS )
000018  2c01              CMP      r4,#1
00001a  d116              BNE      |L42.74|
;;;2089       {
;;;2090           /* freertos_tasks_c_additions_init() should only be called if the user
;;;2091            * definable macro FREERTOS_TASKS_C_ADDITIONS_INIT() is defined, as that is
;;;2092            * the only macro called by the function. */
;;;2093           #ifdef FREERTOS_TASKS_C_ADDITIONS_INIT
;;;2094               {
;;;2095                   freertos_tasks_c_additions_init();
;;;2096               }
;;;2097           #endif
;;;2098   
;;;2099           /* Interrupts are turned off here, to ensure a tick does not occur
;;;2100            * before or during the call to xPortStartScheduler().  The stacks of
;;;2101            * the created tasks contain a status word with interrupts switched on
;;;2102            * so interrupts will automatically get re-enabled when the first task
;;;2103            * starts to run. */
;;;2104           portDISABLE_INTERRUPTS();
00001c  bf00              NOP      
00001e  20bf              MOVS     r0,#0xbf
000020  f3808811          MSR      BASEPRI,r0
000024  f3bf8f4f          DSB      
000028  f3bf8f6f          ISB      
00002c  bf00              NOP      
;;;2105   
;;;2106           #if ( configUSE_NEWLIB_REENTRANT == 1 )
;;;2107               {
;;;2108                   /* Switch Newlib's _impure_ptr variable to point to the _reent
;;;2109                    * structure specific to the task that will run first.
;;;2110                    * See the third party link http://www.nadler.com/embedded/newlibAndFreeRTOS.html
;;;2111                    * for additional information. */
;;;2112                   _impure_ptr = &( pxCurrentTCB->xNewLib_reent );
;;;2113               }
;;;2114           #endif /* configUSE_NEWLIB_REENTRANT */
;;;2115   
;;;2116           xNextTaskUnblockTime = portMAX_DELAY;
00002e  f04f30ff          MOV      r0,#0xffffffff
000032  490b              LDR      r1,|L42.96|
000034  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; xNextTaskUnblockTime
;;;2117           xSchedulerRunning = pdTRUE;
000036  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
000038  490a              LDR      r1,|L42.100|
00003a  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; xSchedulerRunning
;;;2118           xTickCount = ( TickType_t ) configINITIAL_TICK_COUNT;
00003c  2000              MOVS     r0,#0
00003e  490a              LDR      r1,|L42.104|
000040  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; xTickCount
;;;2119   
;;;2120           /* If configGENERATE_RUN_TIME_STATS is defined then the following
;;;2121            * macro must be defined to configure the timer/counter used to generate
;;;2122            * the run time counter time base.   NOTE:  If configGENERATE_RUN_TIME_STATS
;;;2123            * is set to 0 and the following line fails to build then ensure you do not
;;;2124            * have portCONFIGURE_TIMER_FOR_RUN_TIME_STATS() defined in your
;;;2125            * FreeRTOSConfig.h file. */
;;;2126           portCONFIGURE_TIMER_FOR_RUN_TIME_STATS();
000042  490a              LDR      r1,|L42.108|
000044  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; ulHighFrequencyTimerTicks
;;;2127   
;;;2128           traceTASK_SWITCHED_IN();
;;;2129   
;;;2130           /* Setting up the timer tick is hardware specific and thus in the
;;;2131            * portable interface. */
;;;2132           if( xPortStartScheduler() != pdFALSE )
000046  f7fffffe          BL       xPortStartScheduler
                  |L42.74|
;;;2133           {
;;;2134               /* Should not reach here as if the scheduler is running the
;;;2135                * function will not return. */
;;;2136           }
;;;2137           else
;;;2138           {
;;;2139               /* Should only reach here if a task calls xTaskEndScheduler(). */
;;;2140           }
;;;2141       }
;;;2142       else
;;;2143       {
;;;2144           /* This line will only be reached if the kernel could not be started,
;;;2145            * because there was not enough FreeRTOS heap to create the idle task
;;;2146            * or the timer task. */
;;;2147           configASSERT( xReturn != errCOULD_NOT_ALLOCATE_REQUIRED_MEMORY );
;;;2148       }
;;;2149   
;;;2150       /* Prevent compiler warnings if INCLUDE_xTaskGetIdleTaskHandle is set to 0,
;;;2151        * meaning xIdleTaskHandle is not used anywhere else. */
;;;2152       ( void ) xIdleTaskHandle;
;;;2153   
;;;2154       /* OpenOCD makes use of uxTopUsedPriority for thread debugging. Prevent uxTopUsedPriority
;;;2155        * from getting optimized out as it is no longer used by the kernel. */
;;;2156       ( void ) uxTopUsedPriority;
00004a  4809              LDR      r0,|L42.112|
00004c  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; uxTopUsedPriority
;;;2157   }
00004e  bd1c              POP      {r2-r4,pc}
;;;2158   /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
                          ENDP

                  |L42.80|
                          DCD      xIdleTaskHandle
                  |L42.84|
000054  49444c45          DCB      "IDLE",0
000058  00      
000059  00                DCB      0
00005a  00                DCB      0
00005b  00                DCB      0
                  |L42.92|
                          DCD      prvIdleTask
                  |L42.96|
                          DCD      xNextTaskUnblockTime
                  |L42.100|
                          DCD      xSchedulerRunning
                  |L42.104|
                          DCD      xTickCount
                  |L42.108|
                          DCD      ulHighFrequencyTimerTicks
                  |L42.112|
                          DCD      uxTopUsedPriority

                          AREA ||i.vTaskSuspend||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  vTaskSuspend PROC
;;;1750   
;;;1751       void vTaskSuspend( TaskHandle_t xTaskToSuspend )
000000  b570              PUSH     {r4-r6,lr}
;;;1752       {
000002  4605              MOV      r5,r0
;;;1753           TCB_t * pxTCB;
;;;1754   
;;;1755           taskENTER_CRITICAL();
000004  f7fffffe          BL       vPortEnterCritical
;;;1756           {
;;;1757               /* If null is passed in here then it is the running task that is
;;;1758                * being suspended. */
;;;1759               pxTCB = prvGetTCBFromHandle( xTaskToSuspend );
000008  b915              CBNZ     r5,|L43.16|
00000a  482c              LDR      r0,|L43.188|
00000c  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
00000e  e000              B        |L43.18|
                  |L43.16|
000010  4628              MOV      r0,r5
                  |L43.18|
000012  4604              MOV      r4,r0
;;;1760   
;;;1761               traceTASK_SUSPEND( pxTCB );
;;;1762   
;;;1763               /* Remove task from the ready/delayed list and place in the
;;;1764                * suspended list. */
;;;1765               if( uxListRemove( &( pxTCB->xStateListItem ) ) == ( UBaseType_t ) 0 )
000014  1d20              ADDS     r0,r4,#4
000016  f7fffffe          BL       uxListRemove
00001a  b978              CBNZ     r0,|L43.60|
;;;1766               {
;;;1767                   taskRESET_READY_PRIORITY( pxTCB->uxPriority );
00001c  6ae0              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x2c]
00001e  eb000080          ADD      r0,r0,r0,LSL #2
000022  4927              LDR      r1,|L43.192|
000024  f8510020          LDR      r0,[r1,r0,LSL #2]
000028  b940              CBNZ     r0,|L43.60|
00002a  f894102c          LDRB     r1,[r4,#0x2c]
00002e  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
000030  4088              LSLS     r0,r0,r1
000032  4924              LDR      r1,|L43.196|
000034  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
000036  4381              BICS     r1,r1,r0
000038  4822              LDR      r0,|L43.196|
00003a  6001              STR      r1,[r0,#0]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
                  |L43.60|
;;;1768               }
;;;1769               else
;;;1770               {
;;;1771                   mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1772               }
;;;1773   
;;;1774               /* Is the task waiting on an event also? */
;;;1775               if( listLIST_ITEM_CONTAINER( &( pxTCB->xEventListItem ) ) != NULL )
00003c  6aa0              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x28]
00003e  b118              CBZ      r0,|L43.72|
;;;1776               {
;;;1777                   ( void ) uxListRemove( &( pxTCB->xEventListItem ) );
000040  f1040018          ADD      r0,r4,#0x18
000044  f7fffffe          BL       uxListRemove
                  |L43.72|
;;;1778               }
;;;1779               else
;;;1780               {
;;;1781                   mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1782               }
;;;1783   
;;;1784               vListInsertEnd( &xSuspendedTaskList, &( pxTCB->xStateListItem ) );
000048  1d21              ADDS     r1,r4,#4
00004a  481f              LDR      r0,|L43.200|
00004c  f7fffffe          BL       vListInsertEnd
;;;1785   
;;;1786               #if ( configUSE_TASK_NOTIFICATIONS == 1 )
;;;1787                   {
;;;1788                       BaseType_t x;
;;;1789   
;;;1790                       for( x = 0; x < configTASK_NOTIFICATION_ARRAY_ENTRIES; x++ )
000050  2000              MOVS     r0,#0
000052  e009              B        |L43.104|
                  |L43.84|
;;;1791                       {
;;;1792                           if( pxTCB->ucNotifyState[ x ] == taskWAITING_NOTIFICATION )
000054  f104015c          ADD      r1,r4,#0x5c
000058  5c09              LDRB     r1,[r1,r0]
00005a  2901              CMP      r1,#1
00005c  d103              BNE      |L43.102|
;;;1793                           {
;;;1794                               /* The task was blocked to wait for a notification, but is
;;;1795                                * now suspended, so no notification was received. */
;;;1796                               pxTCB->ucNotifyState[ x ] = taskNOT_WAITING_NOTIFICATION;
00005e  2200              MOVS     r2,#0
000060  f104015c          ADD      r1,r4,#0x5c
000064  540a              STRB     r2,[r1,r0]
                  |L43.102|
000066  1c40              ADDS     r0,r0,#1              ;1790
                  |L43.104|
000068  2801              CMP      r0,#1                 ;1790
00006a  dbf3              BLT      |L43.84|
;;;1797                           }
;;;1798                       }
;;;1799                   }
;;;1800               #endif /* if ( configUSE_TASK_NOTIFICATIONS == 1 ) */
;;;1801           }
;;;1802           taskEXIT_CRITICAL();
00006c  f7fffffe          BL       vPortExitCritical
;;;1803   
;;;1804           if( xSchedulerRunning != pdFALSE )
000070  4816              LDR      r0,|L43.204|
000072  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; xSchedulerRunning
000074  b128              CBZ      r0,|L43.130|
;;;1805           {
;;;1806               /* Reset the next expected unblock time in case it referred to the
;;;1807                * task that is now in the Suspended state. */
;;;1808               taskENTER_CRITICAL();
000076  f7fffffe          BL       vPortEnterCritical
;;;1809               {
;;;1810                   prvResetNextTaskUnblockTime();
00007a  f7fffffe          BL       prvResetNextTaskUnblockTime
;;;1811               }
;;;1812               taskEXIT_CRITICAL();
00007e  f7fffffe          BL       vPortExitCritical
                  |L43.130|
;;;1813           }
;;;1814           else
;;;1815           {
;;;1816               mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1817           }
;;;1818   
;;;1819           if( pxTCB == pxCurrentTCB )
000082  480e              LDR      r0,|L43.188|
000084  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000086  4284              CMP      r4,r0
000088  d117              BNE      |L43.186|
;;;1820           {
;;;1821               if( xSchedulerRunning != pdFALSE )
00008a  4810              LDR      r0,|L43.204|
00008c  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; xSchedulerRunning
00008e  b140              CBZ      r0,|L43.162|
;;;1822               {
;;;1823                   /* The current task has just been suspended. */
;;;1824                   configASSERT( uxSchedulerSuspended == 0 );
;;;1825                   portYIELD_WITHIN_API();
000090  f04f5080          MOV      r0,#0x10000000
000094  490e              LDR      r1,|L43.208|
000096  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]
000098  f3bf8f4f          DSB      
00009c  f3bf8f6f          ISB      
0000a0  e00b              B        |L43.186|
                  |L43.162|
;;;1826               }
;;;1827               else
;;;1828               {
;;;1829                   /* The scheduler is not running, but the task that was pointed
;;;1830                    * to by pxCurrentTCB has just been suspended and pxCurrentTCB
;;;1831                    * must be adjusted to point to a different task. */
;;;1832                   if( listCURRENT_LIST_LENGTH( &xSuspendedTaskList ) == uxCurrentNumberOfTasks ) /*lint !e931 Right has no side effect, just volatile. */
0000a2  4809              LDR      r0,|L43.200|
0000a4  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; xSuspendedTaskList
0000a6  490b              LDR      r1,|L43.212|
0000a8  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; uxCurrentNumberOfTasks
0000aa  4288              CMP      r0,r1
0000ac  d103              BNE      |L43.182|
;;;1833                   {
;;;1834                       /* No other tasks are ready, so set pxCurrentTCB back to
;;;1835                        * NULL so when the next task is created pxCurrentTCB will
;;;1836                        * be set to point to it no matter what its relative priority
;;;1837                        * is. */
;;;1838                       pxCurrentTCB = NULL;
0000ae  2000              MOVS     r0,#0
0000b0  4902              LDR      r1,|L43.188|
0000b2  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
0000b4  e001              B        |L43.186|
                  |L43.182|
;;;1839                   }
;;;1840                   else
;;;1841                   {
;;;1842                       vTaskSwitchContext();
0000b6  f7fffffe          BL       vTaskSwitchContext
                  |L43.186|
;;;1843                   }
;;;1844               }
;;;1845           }
;;;1846           else
;;;1847           {
;;;1848               mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1849           }
;;;1850       }
0000ba  bd70              POP      {r4-r6,pc}
;;;1851   
                          ENDP

                  |L43.188|
                          DCD      pxCurrentTCB
                  |L43.192|
                          DCD      pxReadyTasksLists
                  |L43.196|
                          DCD      uxTopReadyPriority
                  |L43.200|
                          DCD      xSuspendedTaskList
                  |L43.204|
                          DCD      xSchedulerRunning
                  |L43.208|
                          DCD      0xe000ed04
                  |L43.212|
                          DCD      uxCurrentNumberOfTasks

                          AREA ||i.vTaskSuspendAll||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  vTaskSuspendAll PROC
;;;2170   
;;;2171   void vTaskSuspendAll( void )
000000  4802              LDR      r0,|L44.12|
;;;2172   {
;;;2173       /* A critical section is not required as the variable is of type
;;;2174        * BaseType_t.  Please read Richard Barry's reply in the following link to a
;;;2175        * post in the FreeRTOS support forum before reporting this as a bug! -
;;;2176        * https://goo.gl/wu4acr */
;;;2177   
;;;2178       /* portSOFRWARE_BARRIER() is only implemented for emulated/simulated ports that
;;;2179        * do not otherwise exhibit real time behaviour. */
;;;2180       portSOFTWARE_BARRIER();
;;;2181   
;;;2182       /* The scheduler is suspended if uxSchedulerSuspended is non-zero.  An increment
;;;2183        * is used to allow calls to vTaskSuspendAll() to nest. */
;;;2184       ++uxSchedulerSuspended;
000002  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; uxSchedulerSuspended
000004  1c40              ADDS     r0,r0,#1
000006  4901              LDR      r1,|L44.12|
000008  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; uxSchedulerSuspended
;;;2185   
;;;2186       /* Enforces ordering for ports and optimised compilers that may otherwise place
;;;2187        * the above increment elsewhere. */
;;;2188       portMEMORY_BARRIER();
;;;2189   }
00000a  4770              BX       lr
;;;2190   /*----------------------------------------------------------*/
                          ENDP

                  |L44.12|
                          DCD      uxSchedulerSuspended

                          AREA ||i.vTaskSwitchContext||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  vTaskSwitchContext PROC
;;;3054   
;;;3055   void vTaskSwitchContext( void )
000000  481f              LDR      r0,|L45.128|
;;;3056   {
;;;3057       if( uxSchedulerSuspended != ( UBaseType_t ) pdFALSE )
000002  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; uxSchedulerSuspended
000004  b118              CBZ      r0,|L45.14|
;;;3058       {
;;;3059           /* The scheduler is currently suspended - do not allow a context
;;;3060            * switch. */
;;;3061           xYieldPending = pdTRUE;
000006  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
000008  491e              LDR      r1,|L45.132|
00000a  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; xYieldPending
00000c  e037              B        |L45.126|
                  |L45.14|
;;;3062       }
;;;3063       else
;;;3064       {
;;;3065           xYieldPending = pdFALSE;
00000e  2000              MOVS     r0,#0
000010  491c              LDR      r1,|L45.132|
000012  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; xYieldPending
;;;3066           traceTASK_SWITCHED_OUT();
;;;3067   
;;;3068           #if ( configGENERATE_RUN_TIME_STATS == 1 )
;;;3069               {
;;;3070                   #ifdef portALT_GET_RUN_TIME_COUNTER_VALUE
;;;3071                       portALT_GET_RUN_TIME_COUNTER_VALUE( ulTotalRunTime );
;;;3072                   #else
;;;3073                       ulTotalRunTime = portGET_RUN_TIME_COUNTER_VALUE();
000014  481c              LDR      r0,|L45.136|
000016  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; ulHighFrequencyTimerTicks
000018  491c              LDR      r1,|L45.140|
00001a  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; ulTotalRunTime
;;;3074                   #endif
;;;3075   
;;;3076                   /* Add the amount of time the task has been running to the
;;;3077                    * accumulated time so far.  The time the task started running was
;;;3078                    * stored in ulTaskSwitchedInTime.  Note that there is no overflow
;;;3079                    * protection here so count values are only valid until the timer
;;;3080                    * overflows.  The guard against negative values is to protect
;;;3081                    * against suspect run time stat counter implementations - which
;;;3082                    * are provided by the application, not the kernel. */
;;;3083                   if( ulTotalRunTime > ulTaskSwitchedInTime )
00001c  4608              MOV      r0,r1
00001e  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; ulTotalRunTime
000020  491b              LDR      r1,|L45.144|
000022  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; ulTaskSwitchedInTime
000024  4288              CMP      r0,r1
000026  d90b              BLS      |L45.64|
;;;3084                   {
;;;3085                       pxCurrentTCB->ulRunTimeCounter += ( ulTotalRunTime - ulTaskSwitchedInTime );
000028  491a              LDR      r1,|L45.148|
00002a  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
00002c  f1010054          ADD      r0,r1,#0x54
000030  6d49              LDR      r1,[r1,#0x54]
000032  4a16              LDR      r2,|L45.140|
000034  6812              LDR      r2,[r2,#0]  ; ulTotalRunTime
000036  4b16              LDR      r3,|L45.144|
000038  681b              LDR      r3,[r3,#0]  ; ulTaskSwitchedInTime
00003a  1ad2              SUBS     r2,r2,r3
00003c  4411              ADD      r1,r1,r2
00003e  6001              STR      r1,[r0,#0]
                  |L45.64|
;;;3086                   }
;;;3087                   else
;;;3088                   {
;;;3089                       mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;3090                   }
;;;3091   
;;;3092                   ulTaskSwitchedInTime = ulTotalRunTime;
000040  4812              LDR      r0,|L45.140|
000042  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; ulTotalRunTime
000044  4912              LDR      r1,|L45.144|
000046  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; ulTaskSwitchedInTime
;;;3093               }
;;;3094           #endif /* configGENERATE_RUN_TIME_STATS */
;;;3095   
;;;3096           /* Check for stack overflow, if configured. */
;;;3097           taskCHECK_FOR_STACK_OVERFLOW();
;;;3098   
;;;3099           /* Before the currently running task is switched out, save its errno. */
;;;3100           #if ( configUSE_POSIX_ERRNO == 1 )
;;;3101               {
;;;3102                   pxCurrentTCB->iTaskErrno = FreeRTOS_errno;
;;;3103               }
;;;3104           #endif
;;;3105   
;;;3106           /* Select a new task to run using either the generic C or port
;;;3107            * optimised asm code. */
;;;3108           taskSELECT_HIGHEST_PRIORITY_TASK(); /*lint !e9079 void * is used as this macro is used with timers and co-routines too.  Alignment is known to be fine as the type of the pointer stored and retrieved is the same. */
000048  4813              LDR      r0,|L45.152|
00004a  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
00004c  fab0f080          CLZ      r0,r0
000050  f1c0011f          RSB      r1,r0,#0x1f
000054  eb010281          ADD      r2,r1,r1,LSL #2
000058  4b10              LDR      r3,|L45.156|
00005a  eb030082          ADD      r0,r3,r2,LSL #2
00005e  6842              LDR      r2,[r0,#4]
000060  6852              LDR      r2,[r2,#4]
000062  6042              STR      r2,[r0,#4]
000064  f1000208          ADD      r2,r0,#8
000068  6843              LDR      r3,[r0,#4]
00006a  4293              CMP      r3,r2
00006c  d102              BNE      |L45.116|
00006e  6842              LDR      r2,[r0,#4]
000070  6852              LDR      r2,[r2,#4]
000072  6042              STR      r2,[r0,#4]
                  |L45.116|
000074  6842              LDR      r2,[r0,#4]
000076  68d2              LDR      r2,[r2,#0xc]
000078  4b06              LDR      r3,|L45.148|
00007a  601a              STR      r2,[r3,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
00007c  bf00              NOP      
                  |L45.126|
;;;3109           traceTASK_SWITCHED_IN();
;;;3110   
;;;3111           /* After the new task is switched in, update the global errno. */
;;;3112           #if ( configUSE_POSIX_ERRNO == 1 )
;;;3113               {
;;;3114                   FreeRTOS_errno = pxCurrentTCB->iTaskErrno;
;;;3115               }
;;;3116           #endif
;;;3117   
;;;3118           #if ( configUSE_NEWLIB_REENTRANT == 1 )
;;;3119               {
;;;3120                   /* Switch Newlib's _impure_ptr variable to point to the _reent
;;;3121                    * structure specific to this task.
;;;3122                    * See the third party link http://www.nadler.com/embedded/newlibAndFreeRTOS.html
;;;3123                    * for additional information. */
;;;3124                   _impure_ptr = &( pxCurrentTCB->xNewLib_reent );
;;;3125               }
;;;3126           #endif /* configUSE_NEWLIB_REENTRANT */
;;;3127       }
;;;3128   }
00007e  4770              BX       lr
;;;3129   /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
                          ENDP

                  |L45.128|
                          DCD      uxSchedulerSuspended
                  |L45.132|
                          DCD      xYieldPending
                  |L45.136|
                          DCD      ulHighFrequencyTimerTicks
                  |L45.140|
                          DCD      ulTotalRunTime
                  |L45.144|
                          DCD      ulTaskSwitchedInTime
                  |L45.148|
                          DCD      pxCurrentTCB
                  |L45.152|
                          DCD      uxTopReadyPriority
                  |L45.156|
                          DCD      pxReadyTasksLists

                          AREA ||i.xTaskCatchUpTicks||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  xTaskCatchUpTicks PROC
;;;2671   
;;;2672   BaseType_t xTaskCatchUpTicks( TickType_t xTicksToCatchUp )
000000  b570              PUSH     {r4-r6,lr}
;;;2673   {
000002  4604              MOV      r4,r0
;;;2674       BaseType_t xYieldOccurred;
;;;2675   
;;;2676       /* Must not be called with the scheduler suspended as the implementation
;;;2677        * relies on xPendedTicks being wound down to 0 in xTaskResumeAll(). */
;;;2678       configASSERT( uxSchedulerSuspended == 0 );
;;;2679   
;;;2680       /* Use xPendedTicks to mimic xTicksToCatchUp number of ticks occurring when
;;;2681        * the scheduler is suspended so the ticks are executed in xTaskResumeAll(). */
;;;2682       vTaskSuspendAll();
000004  f7fffffe          BL       vTaskSuspendAll
;;;2683       xPendedTicks += xTicksToCatchUp;
000008  4804              LDR      r0,|L46.28|
00000a  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; xPendedTicks
00000c  4420              ADD      r0,r0,r4
00000e  4903              LDR      r1,|L46.28|
000010  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; xPendedTicks
;;;2684       xYieldOccurred = xTaskResumeAll();
000012  f7fffffe          BL       xTaskResumeAll
000016  4605              MOV      r5,r0
;;;2685   
;;;2686       return xYieldOccurred;
000018  4628              MOV      r0,r5
;;;2687   }
00001a  bd70              POP      {r4-r6,pc}
;;;2688   /*----------------------------------------------------------*/
                          ENDP

                  |L46.28|
                          DCD      xPendedTicks

                          AREA ||i.xTaskCheckForTimeOut||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  xTaskCheckForTimeOut PROC
;;;3346   
;;;3347   BaseType_t xTaskCheckForTimeOut( TimeOut_t * const pxTimeOut,
000000  b570              PUSH     {r4-r6,lr}
;;;3348                                    TickType_t * const pxTicksToWait )
;;;3349   {
000002  4606              MOV      r6,r0
000004  460c              MOV      r4,r1
;;;3350       BaseType_t xReturn;
;;;3351   
;;;3352       configASSERT( pxTimeOut );
;;;3353       configASSERT( pxTicksToWait );
;;;3354   
;;;3355       taskENTER_CRITICAL();
000006  f7fffffe          BL       vPortEnterCritical
;;;3356       {
;;;3357           /* Minor optimisation.  The tick count cannot change in this block. */
;;;3358           const TickType_t xConstTickCount = xTickCount;
00000a  4813              LDR      r0,|L47.88|
00000c  6802              LDR      r2,[r0,#0]  ; xTickCount
;;;3359           const TickType_t xElapsedTime = xConstTickCount - pxTimeOut->xTimeOnEntering;
00000e  6870              LDR      r0,[r6,#4]
000010  1a13              SUBS     r3,r2,r0
;;;3360   
;;;3361           #if ( INCLUDE_xTaskAbortDelay == 1 )
;;;3362               if( pxCurrentTCB->ucDelayAborted != ( uint8_t ) pdFALSE )
;;;3363               {
;;;3364                   /* The delay was aborted, which is not the same as a time out,
;;;3365                    * but has the same result. */
;;;3366                   pxCurrentTCB->ucDelayAborted = pdFALSE;
;;;3367                   xReturn = pdTRUE;
;;;3368               }
;;;3369               else
;;;3370           #endif
;;;3371   
;;;3372           #if ( INCLUDE_vTaskSuspend == 1 )
;;;3373               if( *pxTicksToWait == portMAX_DELAY )
000012  6820              LDR      r0,[r4,#0]
000014  1c40              ADDS     r0,r0,#1
000016  b908              CBNZ     r0,|L47.28|
;;;3374               {
;;;3375                   /* If INCLUDE_vTaskSuspend is set to 1 and the block time
;;;3376                    * specified is the maximum block time then the task should block
;;;3377                    * indefinitely, and therefore never time out. */
;;;3378                   xReturn = pdFALSE;
000018  2500              MOVS     r5,#0
00001a  e019              B        |L47.80|
                  |L47.28|
;;;3379               }
;;;3380               else
;;;3381           #endif
;;;3382   
;;;3383           if( ( xNumOfOverflows != pxTimeOut->xOverflowCount ) && ( xConstTickCount >= pxTimeOut->xTimeOnEntering ) ) /*lint !e525 Indentation preferred as is to make code within pre-processor directives clearer. */
00001c  490f              LDR      r1,|L47.92|
00001e  6830              LDR      r0,[r6,#0]
000020  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; xNumOfOverflows
000022  4288              CMP      r0,r1
000024  d006              BEQ      |L47.52|
000026  6870              LDR      r0,[r6,#4]
000028  4290              CMP      r0,r2
00002a  d803              BHI      |L47.52|
;;;3384           {
;;;3385               /* The tick count is greater than the time at which
;;;3386                * vTaskSetTimeout() was called, but has also overflowed since
;;;3387                * vTaskSetTimeOut() was called.  It must have wrapped all the way
;;;3388                * around and gone past again. This passed since vTaskSetTimeout()
;;;3389                * was called. */
;;;3390               xReturn = pdTRUE;
00002c  2501              MOVS     r5,#1
;;;3391               *pxTicksToWait = ( TickType_t ) 0;
00002e  2000              MOVS     r0,#0
000030  6020              STR      r0,[r4,#0]
000032  e00d              B        |L47.80|
                  |L47.52|
;;;3392           }
;;;3393           else if( xElapsedTime < *pxTicksToWait ) /*lint !e961 Explicit casting is only redundant with some compilers, whereas others require it to prevent integer conversion errors. */
000034  6820              LDR      r0,[r4,#0]
000036  4298              CMP      r0,r3
000038  d907              BLS      |L47.74|
;;;3394           {
;;;3395               /* Not a genuine timeout. Adjust parameters for time remaining. */
;;;3396               *pxTicksToWait -= xElapsedTime;
00003a  6820              LDR      r0,[r4,#0]
00003c  1ac0              SUBS     r0,r0,r3
00003e  6020              STR      r0,[r4,#0]
;;;3397               vTaskInternalSetTimeOutState( pxTimeOut );
000040  4630              MOV      r0,r6
000042  f7fffffe          BL       vTaskInternalSetTimeOutState
;;;3398               xReturn = pdFALSE;
000046  2500              MOVS     r5,#0
000048  e002              B        |L47.80|
                  |L47.74|
;;;3399           }
;;;3400           else
;;;3401           {
;;;3402               *pxTicksToWait = ( TickType_t ) 0;
00004a  2000              MOVS     r0,#0
00004c  6020              STR      r0,[r4,#0]
;;;3403               xReturn = pdTRUE;
00004e  2501              MOVS     r5,#1
                  |L47.80|
;;;3404           }
;;;3405       }
;;;3406       taskEXIT_CRITICAL();
000050  f7fffffe          BL       vPortExitCritical
;;;3407   
;;;3408       return xReturn;
000054  4628              MOV      r0,r5
;;;3409   }
000056  bd70              POP      {r4-r6,pc}
;;;3410   /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
                          ENDP

                  |L47.88|
                          DCD      xTickCount
                  |L47.92|
                          DCD      xNumOfOverflows

                          AREA ||i.xTaskCreate||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=1

                  xTaskCreate PROC
;;;770    
;;;771        BaseType_t xTaskCreate( TaskFunction_t pxTaskCode,
000000  e92d4fff          PUSH     {r0-r11,lr}
;;;772                                const char * const pcName, /*lint !e971 Unqualified char types are allowed for strings and single characters only. */
;;;773                                const configSTACK_DEPTH_TYPE usStackDepth,
;;;774                                void * const pvParameters,
;;;775                                UBaseType_t uxPriority,
;;;776                                TaskHandle_t * const pxCreatedTask )
;;;777        {
000004  b085              SUB      sp,sp,#0x14
000006  4680              MOV      r8,r0
000008  4689              MOV      r9,r1
00000a  4617              MOV      r7,r2
00000c  e9ddab12          LDRD     r10,r11,[sp,#0x48]
;;;778            TCB_t * pxNewTCB;
;;;779            BaseType_t xReturn;
;;;780    
;;;781            /* If the stack grows down then allocate the stack then the TCB so the stack
;;;782             * does not grow into the TCB.  Likewise if the stack grows up then allocate
;;;783             * the TCB then the stack. */
;;;784            #if ( portSTACK_GROWTH > 0 )
;;;785                {
;;;786                    /* Allocate space for the TCB.  Where the memory comes from depends on
;;;787                     * the implementation of the port malloc function and whether or not static
;;;788                     * allocation is being used. */
;;;789                    pxNewTCB = ( TCB_t * ) pvPortMalloc( sizeof( TCB_t ) );
;;;790    
;;;791                    if( pxNewTCB != NULL )
;;;792                    {
;;;793                        /* Allocate space for the stack used by the task being created.
;;;794                         * The base of the stack memory stored in the TCB so the task can
;;;795                         * be deleted later if required. */
;;;796                        pxNewTCB->pxStack = ( StackType_t * ) pvPortMalloc( ( ( ( size_t ) usStackDepth ) * sizeof( StackType_t ) ) ); /*lint !e961 MISRA exception as the casts are only redundant for some ports. */
;;;797    
;;;798                        if( pxNewTCB->pxStack == NULL )
;;;799                        {
;;;800                            /* Could not allocate the stack.  Delete the allocated TCB. */
;;;801                            vPortFree( pxNewTCB );
;;;802                            pxNewTCB = NULL;
;;;803                        }
;;;804                    }
;;;805                }
;;;806            #else /* portSTACK_GROWTH */
;;;807                {
;;;808                    StackType_t * pxStack;
;;;809    
;;;810                    /* Allocate space for the stack used by the task being created. */
;;;811                    pxStack = pvPortMalloc( ( ( ( size_t ) usStackDepth ) * sizeof( StackType_t ) ) ); /*lint !e9079 All values returned by pvPortMalloc() have at least the alignment required by the MCU's stack and this allocation is the stack. */
000010  00b8              LSLS     r0,r7,#2
000012  f7fffffe          BL       pvPortMalloc
000016  4606              MOV      r6,r0
;;;812    
;;;813                    if( pxStack != NULL )
000018  b156              CBZ      r6,|L48.48|
;;;814                    {
;;;815                        /* Allocate space for the TCB. */
;;;816                        pxNewTCB = ( TCB_t * ) pvPortMalloc( sizeof( TCB_t ) ); /*lint !e9087 !e9079 All values returned by pvPortMalloc() have at least the alignment required by the MCU's stack, and the first member of TCB_t is always a pointer to the task's stack. */
00001a  2060              MOVS     r0,#0x60
00001c  f7fffffe          BL       pvPortMalloc
000020  4604              MOV      r4,r0
;;;817    
;;;818                        if( pxNewTCB != NULL )
000022  b10c              CBZ      r4,|L48.40|
;;;819                        {
;;;820                            /* Store the stack location in the TCB. */
;;;821                            pxNewTCB->pxStack = pxStack;
000024  6326              STR      r6,[r4,#0x30]
000026  e004              B        |L48.50|
                  |L48.40|
;;;822                        }
;;;823                        else
;;;824                        {
;;;825                            /* The stack cannot be used as the TCB was not created.  Free
;;;826                             * it again. */
;;;827                            vPortFree( pxStack );
000028  4630              MOV      r0,r6
00002a  f7fffffe          BL       vPortFree
00002e  e000              B        |L48.50|
                  |L48.48|
;;;828                        }
;;;829                    }
;;;830                    else
;;;831                    {
;;;832                        pxNewTCB = NULL;
000030  2400              MOVS     r4,#0
                  |L48.50|
;;;833                    }
;;;834                }
;;;835            #endif /* portSTACK_GROWTH */
;;;836    
;;;837            if( pxNewTCB != NULL )
000032  b17c              CBZ      r4,|L48.84|
;;;838            {
;;;839                #if ( tskSTATIC_AND_DYNAMIC_ALLOCATION_POSSIBLE != 0 ) /*lint !e9029 !e731 Macro has been consolidated for readability reasons. */
;;;840                    {
;;;841                        /* Tasks can be created statically or dynamically, so note this
;;;842                         * task was created dynamically in case it is later deleted. */
;;;843                        pxNewTCB->ucStaticallyAllocated = tskDYNAMICALLY_ALLOCATED_STACK_AND_TCB;
;;;844                    }
;;;845                #endif /* tskSTATIC_AND_DYNAMIC_ALLOCATION_POSSIBLE */
;;;846    
;;;847                prvInitialiseNewTask( pxTaskCode, pcName, ( uint32_t ) usStackDepth, pvParameters, uxPriority, pxCreatedTask, pxNewTCB, NULL );
000034  2000              MOVS     r0,#0
000036  e9cdab00          STRD     r10,r11,[sp,#0]
00003a  e9cd4002          STRD     r4,r0,[sp,#8]
00003e  463a              MOV      r2,r7
000040  4649              MOV      r1,r9
000042  4640              MOV      r0,r8
000044  9b08              LDR      r3,[sp,#0x20]
000046  f7fffffe          BL       prvInitialiseNewTask
;;;848                prvAddNewTaskToReadyList( pxNewTCB );
00004a  4620              MOV      r0,r4
00004c  f7fffffe          BL       prvAddNewTaskToReadyList
;;;849                xReturn = pdPASS;
000050  2501              MOVS     r5,#1
000052  e001              B        |L48.88|
                  |L48.84|
;;;850            }
;;;851            else
;;;852            {
;;;853                xReturn = errCOULD_NOT_ALLOCATE_REQUIRED_MEMORY;
000054  f04f35ff          MOV      r5,#0xffffffff
                  |L48.88|
;;;854            }
;;;855    
;;;856            return xReturn;
000058  4628              MOV      r0,r5
;;;857        }
00005a  b009              ADD      sp,sp,#0x24
00005c  e8bd8ff0          POP      {r4-r11,pc}
;;;858    
                          ENDP


                          AREA ||i.xTaskDelayUntil||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  xTaskDelayUntil PROC
;;;1293   
;;;1294       BaseType_t xTaskDelayUntil( TickType_t * const pxPreviousWakeTime,
000000  e92d47f0          PUSH     {r4-r10,lr}
;;;1295                                   const TickType_t xTimeIncrement )
;;;1296       {
000004  4604              MOV      r4,r0
000006  4688              MOV      r8,r1
;;;1297           TickType_t xTimeToWake;
;;;1298           BaseType_t xAlreadyYielded, xShouldDelay = pdFALSE;
000008  f04f0900          MOV      r9,#0
;;;1299   
;;;1300           configASSERT( pxPreviousWakeTime );
;;;1301           configASSERT( ( xTimeIncrement > 0U ) );
;;;1302           configASSERT( uxSchedulerSuspended == 0 );
;;;1303   
;;;1304           vTaskSuspendAll();
00000c  f7fffffe          BL       vTaskSuspendAll
;;;1305           {
;;;1306               /* Minor optimisation.  The tick count cannot change in this
;;;1307                * block. */
;;;1308               const TickType_t xConstTickCount = xTickCount;
000010  4816              LDR      r0,|L49.108|
000012  6806              LDR      r6,[r0,#0]  ; xTickCount
;;;1309   
;;;1310               /* Generate the tick time at which the task wants to wake. */
;;;1311               xTimeToWake = *pxPreviousWakeTime + xTimeIncrement;
000014  6820              LDR      r0,[r4,#0]
000016  eb000508          ADD      r5,r0,r8
;;;1312   
;;;1313               if( xConstTickCount < *pxPreviousWakeTime )
00001a  6820              LDR      r0,[r4,#0]
00001c  42b0              CMP      r0,r6
00001e  d907              BLS      |L49.48|
;;;1314               {
;;;1315                   /* The tick count has overflowed since this function was
;;;1316                    * lasted called.  In this case the only time we should ever
;;;1317                    * actually delay is if the wake time has also  overflowed,
;;;1318                    * and the wake time is greater than the tick time.  When this
;;;1319                    * is the case it is as if neither time had overflowed. */
;;;1320                   if( ( xTimeToWake < *pxPreviousWakeTime ) && ( xTimeToWake > xConstTickCount ) )
000020  6820              LDR      r0,[r4,#0]
000022  42a8              CMP      r0,r5
000024  d90b              BLS      |L49.62|
000026  42b5              CMP      r5,r6
000028  d909              BLS      |L49.62|
;;;1321                   {
;;;1322                       xShouldDelay = pdTRUE;
00002a  f04f0901          MOV      r9,#1
00002e  e006              B        |L49.62|
                  |L49.48|
;;;1323                   }
;;;1324                   else
;;;1325                   {
;;;1326                       mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1327                   }
;;;1328               }
;;;1329               else
;;;1330               {
;;;1331                   /* The tick time has not overflowed.  In this case we will
;;;1332                    * delay if either the wake time has overflowed, and/or the
;;;1333                    * tick time is less than the wake time. */
;;;1334                   if( ( xTimeToWake < *pxPreviousWakeTime ) || ( xTimeToWake > xConstTickCount ) )
000030  6820              LDR      r0,[r4,#0]
000032  42a8              CMP      r0,r5
000034  d801              BHI      |L49.58|
000036  42b5              CMP      r5,r6
000038  d901              BLS      |L49.62|
                  |L49.58|
;;;1335                   {
;;;1336                       xShouldDelay = pdTRUE;
00003a  f04f0901          MOV      r9,#1
                  |L49.62|
;;;1337                   }
;;;1338                   else
;;;1339                   {
;;;1340                       mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1341                   }
;;;1342               }
;;;1343   
;;;1344               /* Update the wake time ready for the next call. */
;;;1345               *pxPreviousWakeTime = xTimeToWake;
00003e  6025              STR      r5,[r4,#0]
;;;1346   
;;;1347               if( xShouldDelay != pdFALSE )
000040  f1b90f00          CMP      r9,#0
000044  d003              BEQ      |L49.78|
;;;1348               {
;;;1349                   traceTASK_DELAY_UNTIL( xTimeToWake );
;;;1350   
;;;1351                   /* prvAddCurrentTaskToDelayedList() needs the block time, not
;;;1352                    * the time to wake, so subtract the current tick count. */
;;;1353                   prvAddCurrentTaskToDelayedList( xTimeToWake - xConstTickCount, pdFALSE );
000046  1ba8              SUBS     r0,r5,r6
000048  2100              MOVS     r1,#0
00004a  f7fffffe          BL       prvAddCurrentTaskToDelayedList
                  |L49.78|
;;;1354               }
;;;1355               else
;;;1356               {
;;;1357                   mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1358               }
;;;1359           }
;;;1360           xAlreadyYielded = xTaskResumeAll();
00004e  f7fffffe          BL       xTaskResumeAll
000052  4607              MOV      r7,r0
;;;1361   
;;;1362           /* Force a reschedule if xTaskResumeAll has not already done so, we may
;;;1363            * have put ourselves to sleep. */
;;;1364           if( xAlreadyYielded == pdFALSE )
000054  b93f              CBNZ     r7,|L49.102|
;;;1365           {
;;;1366               portYIELD_WITHIN_API();
000056  f04f5080          MOV      r0,#0x10000000
00005a  4905              LDR      r1,|L49.112|
00005c  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]
00005e  f3bf8f4f          DSB      
000062  f3bf8f6f          ISB      
                  |L49.102|
;;;1367           }
;;;1368           else
;;;1369           {
;;;1370               mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1371           }
;;;1372   
;;;1373           return xShouldDelay;
000066  4648              MOV      r0,r9
;;;1374       }
000068  e8bd87f0          POP      {r4-r10,pc}
;;;1375   
                          ENDP

                  |L49.108|
                          DCD      xTickCount
                  |L49.112|
                          DCD      0xe000ed04

                          AREA ||i.xTaskGenericNotify||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  xTaskGenericNotify PROC
;;;4886   
;;;4887       BaseType_t xTaskGenericNotify( TaskHandle_t xTaskToNotify,
000000  e92d5ff0          PUSH     {r4-r12,lr}
;;;4888                                      UBaseType_t uxIndexToNotify,
;;;4889                                      uint32_t ulValue,
;;;4890                                      eNotifyAction eAction,
;;;4891                                      uint32_t * pulPreviousNotificationValue )
;;;4892       {

000006  460d              MOV      r5,r1
000008  4616              MOV      r6,r2
00000a  461f              MOV      r7,r3
00000c  f8dda028          LDR      r10,[sp,#0x28]
;;;4893           TCB_t * pxTCB;
;;;4894           BaseType_t xReturn = pdPASS;
000010  f04f0b01          MOV      r11,#1
;;;4895           uint8_t ucOriginalNotifyState;
;;;4896

;;;4900   
;;;4901           taskENTER_CRITICAL();

;;;4902           {

00001a  f1ba0f00          CMP      r10,#0
00001e  d005              BEQ      |L50.44|
;;;4904               {
;;;4905                   *pulPreviousNotificationValue = pxTCB->ulNotifiedValue[ uxIndexToNotify ];

000024  f8500025          LDR      r0,[r0,r5,LSL #2]
000028  f8ca0000          STR      r0,[r10,#0]
                  |L50.44|
;;;4906               }
;;;4907   
;;;4908               ucOriginalNotifyState = pxTCB->ucNotifyState[ uxIndexToNotify ];

;;;4909   
;;;4910               pxTCB->ucNotifyState[ uxIndexToNotify ] = taskNOTIFICATION_RECEIVED;

000036  5541              STRB     r1,[r0,r5]
;;;4911   
;;;4912               switch( eAction )

00003a  d229              BCS      |L50.144|
00003c  e8dff007          TBB      [pc,r7]
