                          AREA ||i.xTaskGetTickCount||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  xTaskGetTickCount PROC
;;;2365   
;;;2366   TickType_t xTaskGetTickCount( void )
000000  4901              LDR      r1,|L55.8|
;;;2367   {
;;;2368       TickType_t xTicks;
;;;2369   
;;;2370       /* Critical section required if running on a 16 bit processor. */
;;;2371       portTICK_TYPE_ENTER_CRITICAL();
;;;2372       {
;;;2373           xTicks = xTickCount;
000002  6808              LDR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; xTickCount
;;;2374       }
;;;2375       portTICK_TYPE_EXIT_CRITICAL();
;;;2376   
;;;2377       return xTicks;
;;;2378   }
000004  4770              BX       lr
;;;2379   /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
                          ENDP

000006  0000              DCW      0x0000
                  |L55.8|
                          DCD      xTickCount

                          AREA ||i.xTaskGetTickCountFromISR||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  xTaskGetTickCountFromISR PROC
;;;2380   
;;;2381   TickType_t xTaskGetTickCountFromISR( void )
000000  2100              MOVS     r1,#0
;;;2382   {
;;;2383       TickType_t xReturn;
;;;2384       UBaseType_t uxSavedInterruptStatus;
;;;2385   
;;;2386       /* RTOS ports that support interrupt nesting have the concept of a maximum
;;;2387        * system call (or maximum API call) interrupt priority.  Interrupts that are
;;;2388        * above the maximum system call priority are kept permanently enabled, even
;;;2389        * when the RTOS kernel is in a critical section, but cannot make any calls to
;;;2390        * FreeRTOS API functions.  If configASSERT() is defined in FreeRTOSConfig.h
;;;2391        * then portASSERT_IF_INTERRUPT_PRIORITY_INVALID() will result in an assertion
;;;2392        * failure if a FreeRTOS API function is called from an interrupt that has been
;;;2393        * assigned a priority above the configured maximum system call priority.
;;;2394        * Only FreeRTOS functions that end in FromISR can be called from interrupts
;;;2395        * that have been assigned a priority at or (logically) below the maximum
;;;2396        * system call  interrupt priority.  FreeRTOS maintains a separate interrupt
;;;2397        * safe API to ensure interrupt entry is as fast and as simple as possible.
;;;2398        * More information (albeit Cortex-M specific) is provided on the following
;;;2399        * link: https://www.FreeRTOS.org/RTOS-Cortex-M3-M4.html */
;;;2400       portASSERT_IF_INTERRUPT_PRIORITY_INVALID();
;;;2401   
;;;2402       uxSavedInterruptStatus = portTICK_TYPE_SET_INTERRUPT_MASK_FROM_ISR();
;;;2403       {
;;;2404           xReturn = xTickCount;
000002  4a01              LDR      r2,|L56.8|
000004  6810              LDR      r0,[r2,#0]  ; xTickCount
;;;2405       }
;;;2406       portTICK_TYPE_CLEAR_INTERRUPT_MASK_FROM_ISR( uxSavedInterruptStatus );
;;;2407   
;;;2408       return xReturn;
;;;2409   }
000006  4770              BX       lr
;;;2410   /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
                          ENDP

                  |L56.8|
                          DCD      xTickCount

                          AREA ||i.xTaskIncrementTick||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  xTaskIncrementTick PROC
;;;2769   
;;;2770   BaseType_t xTaskIncrementTick( void )
000000  e92d41f0          PUSH     {r4-r8,lr}
;;;2771   {
;;;2772       TCB_t * pxTCB;
;;;2773       TickType_t xItemValue;
;;;2774       BaseType_t xSwitchRequired = pdFALSE;
000004  2700              MOVS     r7,#0
;;;2775   
;;;2776       /* Called by the portable layer each time a tick interrupt occurs.
;;;2777        * Increments the tick then checks to see if the new tick value will cause any
;;;2778        * tasks to be unblocked. */
;;;2779       traceTASK_INCREMENT_TICK( xTickCount );
;;;2780   
;;;2781       if( uxSchedulerSuspended == ( UBaseType_t ) pdFALSE )
000006  483a              LDR      r0,|L57.240|
000008  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; uxSchedulerSuspended
00000a  2800              CMP      r0,#0
00000c  d167              BNE      |L57.222|
;;;2782       {
;;;2783           /* Minor optimisation.  The tick count cannot change in this
;;;2784            * block. */
;;;2785           const TickType_t xConstTickCount = xTickCount + ( TickType_t ) 1;
00000e  4839              LDR      r0,|L57.244|
000010  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; xTickCount
000012  1c45              ADDS     r5,r0,#1
;;;2786   
;;;2787           /* Increment the RTOS tick, switching the delayed and overflowed
;;;2788            * delayed lists if it wraps to 0. */
;;;2789           xTickCount = xConstTickCount;
000014  4837              LDR      r0,|L57.244|
000016  6005              STR      r5,[r0,#0]  ; xTickCount
;;;2790   
;;;2791           if( xConstTickCount == ( TickType_t ) 0U ) /*lint !e774 'if' does not always evaluate to false as it is looking for an overflow. */
000018  b98d              CBNZ     r5,|L57.62|
;;;2792           {
;;;2793               taskSWITCH_DELAYED_LISTS();
00001a  4837              LDR      r0,|L57.248|
00001c  f8d08000          LDR      r8,[r0,#0]  ; pxDelayedTaskList
000020  4836              LDR      r0,|L57.252|
000022  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; pxOverflowDelayedTaskList
000024  4934              LDR      r1,|L57.248|
000026  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; pxDelayedTaskList
000028  4834              LDR      r0,|L57.252|
00002a  f8c08000          STR      r8,[r0,#0]  ; pxOverflowDelayedTaskList
00002e  4834              LDR      r0,|L57.256|
000030  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; xNumOfOverflows
000032  1c40              ADDS     r0,r0,#1
000034  4932              LDR      r1,|L57.256|
000036  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; xNumOfOverflows
000038  f7fffffe          BL       prvResetNextTaskUnblockTime
00003c  bf00              NOP      
                  |L57.62|
;;;2794           }
;;;2795           else
;;;2796           {
;;;2797               mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;2798           }
;;;2799   
;;;2800           /* See if this tick has made a timeout expire.  Tasks are stored in
;;;2801            * the  queue in the order of their wake time - meaning once one task
;;;2802            * has been found whose block time has not expired there is no need to
;;;2803            * look any further down the list. */
;;;2804           if( xConstTickCount >= xNextTaskUnblockTime )
00003e  4831              LDR      r0,|L57.260|
000040  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; xNextTaskUnblockTime
000042  4285              CMP      r5,r0
000044  d33b              BCC      |L57.190|
;;;2805           {
;;;2806               for( ; ; )
000046  bf00              NOP      
                  |L57.72|
;;;2807               {
;;;2808                   if( listLIST_IS_EMPTY( pxDelayedTaskList ) != pdFALSE )
000048  482b              LDR      r0,|L57.248|
00004a  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; pxDelayedTaskList
00004c  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]
00004e  b908              CBNZ     r0,|L57.84|
000050  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
000052  e000              B        |L57.86|
                  |L57.84|
000054  2000              MOVS     r0,#0
                  |L57.86|
000056  b120              CBZ      r0,|L57.98|
;;;2809                   {
;;;2810                       /* The delayed list is empty.  Set xNextTaskUnblockTime
;;;2811                        * to the maximum possible value so it is extremely
;;;2812                        * unlikely that the
;;;2813                        * if( xTickCount >= xNextTaskUnblockTime ) test will pass
;;;2814                        * next time through. */
;;;2815                       xNextTaskUnblockTime = portMAX_DELAY; /*lint !e961 MISRA exception as the casts are only redundant for some ports. */
000058  f04f30ff          MOV      r0,#0xffffffff
00005c  4929              LDR      r1,|L57.260|
00005e  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; xNextTaskUnblockTime
;;;2816                       break;
000060  e02c              B        |L57.188|
                  |L57.98|
;;;2817                   }
;;;2818                   else
;;;2819                   {
;;;2820                       /* The delayed list is not empty, get the value of the
;;;2821                        * item at the head of the delayed list.  This is the time
;;;2822                        * at which the task at the head of the delayed list must
;;;2823                        * be removed from the Blocked state. */
;;;2824                       pxTCB = listGET_OWNER_OF_HEAD_ENTRY( pxDelayedTaskList ); /*lint !e9079 void * is used as this macro is used with timers and co-routines too.  Alignment is known to be fine as the type of the pointer stored and retrieved is the same. */
000062  4825              LDR      r0,|L57.248|
000064  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; pxDelayedTaskList
000066  68c0              LDR      r0,[r0,#0xc]
000068  68c4              LDR      r4,[r0,#0xc]
;;;2825                       xItemValue = listGET_LIST_ITEM_VALUE( &( pxTCB->xStateListItem ) );
00006a  6866              LDR      r6,[r4,#4]
;;;2826   
;;;2827                       if( xConstTickCount < xItemValue )
00006c  42b5              CMP      r5,r6
00006e  d202              BCS      |L57.118|
;;;2828                       {
;;;2829                           /* It is not time to unblock this item yet, but the
;;;2830                            * item value is the time at which the task at the head
;;;2831                            * of the blocked list must be removed from the Blocked
;;;2832                            * state -  so record the item value in
;;;2833                            * xNextTaskUnblockTime. */
;;;2834                           xNextTaskUnblockTime = xItemValue;
000070  4824              LDR      r0,|L57.260|
000072  6006              STR      r6,[r0,#0]  ; xNextTaskUnblockTime
;;;2835                           break; /*lint !e9011 Code structure here is deedmed easier to understand with multiple breaks. */
000074  e022              B        |L57.188|
                  |L57.118|
;;;2836                       }
;;;2837                       else
;;;2838                       {
;;;2839                           mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;2840                       }
;;;2841   
;;;2842                       /* It is time to remove the item from the Blocked state. */
;;;2843                       ( void ) uxListRemove( &( pxTCB->xStateListItem ) );
000076  1d20              ADDS     r0,r4,#4
000078  f7fffffe          BL       uxListRemove
;;;2844   
;;;2845                       /* Is the task waiting on an event also?  If so remove
;;;2846                        * it from the event list. */
;;;2847                       if( listLIST_ITEM_CONTAINER( &( pxTCB->xEventListItem ) ) != NULL )
00007c  6aa0              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x28]
00007e  b118              CBZ      r0,|L57.136|
;;;2848                       {
;;;2849                           ( void ) uxListRemove( &( pxTCB->xEventListItem ) );
000080  f1040018          ADD      r0,r4,#0x18
000084  f7fffffe          BL       uxListRemove
                  |L57.136|
;;;2850                       }
;;;2851                       else
;;;2852                       {
;;;2853                           mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;2854                       }
;;;2855   
;;;2856                       /* Place the unblocked task into the appropriate ready
;;;2857                        * list. */
;;;2858                       prvAddTaskToReadyList( pxTCB );
000088  f894102c          LDRB     r1,[r4,#0x2c]
00008c  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
00008e  4088              LSLS     r0,r0,r1
000090  491d              LDR      r1,|L57.264|
000092  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
000094  4308              ORRS     r0,r0,r1
000096  491c              LDR      r1,|L57.264|
000098  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
00009a  6ae1              LDR      r1,[r4,#0x2c]
00009c  eb010181          ADD      r1,r1,r1,LSL #2
0000a0  4a1a              LDR      r2,|L57.268|
0000a2  eb020081          ADD      r0,r2,r1,LSL #2
0000a6  1d21              ADDS     r1,r4,#4
0000a8  f7fffffe          BL       vListInsertEnd
;;;2859   
;;;2860                       /* A task being unblocked cannot cause an immediate
;;;2861                        * context switch if preemption is turned off. */
;;;2862                       #if ( configUSE_PREEMPTION == 1 )
;;;2863                           {
;;;2864                               /* Preemption is on, but a context switch should
;;;2865                                * only be performed if the unblocked task has a
;;;2866                                * priority that is equal to or higher than the
;;;2867                                * currently executing task. */
;;;2868                               if( pxTCB->uxPriority >= pxCurrentTCB->uxPriority )
0000ac  4918              LDR      r1,|L57.272|
0000ae  6ae0              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x2c]
0000b0  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
0000b2  6ac9              LDR      r1,[r1,#0x2c]
0000b4  4288              CMP      r0,r1
0000b6  d3c7              BCC      |L57.72|
;;;2869                               {
;;;2870                                   xSwitchRequired = pdTRUE;
0000b8  2701              MOVS     r7,#1
0000ba  e7c5              B        |L57.72|
                  |L57.188|
0000bc  bf00              NOP                            ;2816
                  |L57.190|
;;;2871                               }
;;;2872                               else
;;;2873                               {
;;;2874                                   mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;2875                               }
;;;2876                           }
;;;2877                       #endif /* configUSE_PREEMPTION */
;;;2878                   }
;;;2879               }
;;;2880           }
;;;2881   
;;;2882           /* Tasks of equal priority to the currently running task will share
;;;2883            * processing time (time slice) if preemption is on, and the application
;;;2884            * writer has not explicitly turned time slicing off. */
;;;2885           #if ( ( configUSE_PREEMPTION == 1 ) && ( configUSE_TIME_SLICING == 1 ) )
;;;2886               {
;;;2887                   if( listCURRENT_LIST_LENGTH( &( pxReadyTasksLists[ pxCurrentTCB->uxPriority ] ) ) > ( UBaseType_t ) 1 )
0000be  4814              LDR      r0,|L57.272|
0000c0  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
0000c2  6ac0              LDR      r0,[r0,#0x2c]
0000c4  eb000080          ADD      r0,r0,r0,LSL #2
0000c8  4910              LDR      r1,|L57.268|
0000ca  f8510020          LDR      r0,[r1,r0,LSL #2]
0000ce  2801              CMP      r0,#1
0000d0  d900              BLS      |L57.212|
;;;2888                   {
;;;2889                       xSwitchRequired = pdTRUE;
0000d2  2701              MOVS     r7,#1
                  |L57.212|
;;;2890                   }
;;;2891                   else
;;;2892                   {
;;;2893                       mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;2894                   }
;;;2895               }
;;;2896           #endif /* ( ( configUSE_PREEMPTION == 1 ) && ( configUSE_TIME_SLICING == 1 ) ) */
;;;2897   
;;;2898           #if ( configUSE_TICK_HOOK == 1 )
;;;2899               {
;;;2900                   /* Guard against the tick hook being called when the pended tick
;;;2901                    * count is being unwound (when the scheduler is being unlocked). */
;;;2902                   if( xPendedTicks == ( TickType_t ) 0 )
;;;2903                   {
;;;2904                       vApplicationTickHook();
;;;2905                   }
;;;2906                   else
;;;2907                   {
;;;2908                       mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;2909                   }
;;;2910               }
;;;2911           #endif /* configUSE_TICK_HOOK */
;;;2912   
;;;2913           #if ( configUSE_PREEMPTION == 1 )
;;;2914               {
;;;2915                   if( xYieldPending != pdFALSE )
0000d4  480f              LDR      r0,|L57.276|
0000d6  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; xYieldPending
0000d8  b100              CBZ      r0,|L57.220|
;;;2916                   {
;;;2917                       xSwitchRequired = pdTRUE;
0000da  2701              MOVS     r7,#1
                  |L57.220|
;;;2918                   }
;;;2919                   else
;;;2920                   {
;;;2921                       mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;2922                   }
;;;2923               }
;;;2924           #endif /* configUSE_PREEMPTION */
;;;2925       }
0000dc  e004              B        |L57.232|
                  |L57.222|
;;;2926       else
;;;2927       {
;;;2928           ++xPendedTicks;
0000de  480e              LDR      r0,|L57.280|
0000e0  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; xPendedTicks
0000e2  1c40              ADDS     r0,r0,#1
0000e4  490c              LDR      r1,|L57.280|
0000e6  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; xPendedTicks
                  |L57.232|
;;;2929   
;;;2930           /* The tick hook gets called at regular intervals, even if the
;;;2931            * scheduler is locked. */
;;;2932           #if ( configUSE_TICK_HOOK == 1 )
;;;2933               {
;;;2934                   vApplicationTickHook();
;;;2935               }
;;;2936           #endif
;;;2937       }
;;;2938   
;;;2939       return xSwitchRequired;
0000e8  4638              MOV      r0,r7
;;;2940   }
0000ea  e8bd81f0          POP      {r4-r8,pc}
;;;2941   /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
                          ENDP

0000ee  0000              DCW      0x0000
                  |L57.240|
                          DCD      uxSchedulerSuspended
                  |L57.244|
                          DCD      xTickCount
                  |L57.248|
                          DCD      pxDelayedTaskList
                  |L57.252|
                          DCD      pxOverflowDelayedTaskList
                  |L57.256|
                          DCD      xNumOfOverflows
                  |L57.260|
                          DCD      xNextTaskUnblockTime
                  |L57.264|
                          DCD      uxTopReadyPriority
                  |L57.268|
                          DCD      pxReadyTasksLists
                  |L57.272|
                          DCD      pxCurrentTCB
                  |L57.276|
                          DCD      xYieldPending
                  |L57.280|
                          DCD      xPendedTicks

                          AREA ||i.xTaskPriorityDisinherit||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  xTaskPriorityDisinherit PROC
;;;4190   
;;;4191       BaseType_t xTaskPriorityDisinherit( TaskHandle_t const pxMutexHolder )
000000  b570              PUSH     {r4-r6,lr}
;;;4192       {
000002  4605              MOV      r5,r0
;;;4193           TCB_t * const pxTCB = pxMutexHolder;
000004  462c              MOV      r4,r5
;;;4194           BaseType_t xReturn = pdFALSE;
000006  2600              MOVS     r6,#0
;;;4195   
;;;4196           if( pxMutexHolder != NULL )
000008  b375              CBZ      r5,|L58.104|
;;;4197           {
;;;4198               /* A task can only have an inherited priority if it holds the mutex.
;;;4199                * If the mutex is held by a task then it cannot be given from an
;;;4200                * interrupt, and if a mutex is given by the holding task then it must
;;;4201                * be the running state task. */
;;;4202               configASSERT( pxTCB == pxCurrentTCB );
;;;4203               configASSERT( pxTCB->uxMutexesHeld );
;;;4204               ( pxTCB->uxMutexesHeld )--;
00000a  6d20              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x50]
00000c  1e40              SUBS     r0,r0,#1
00000e  6520              STR      r0,[r4,#0x50]
;;;4205   
;;;4206               /* Has the holder of the mutex inherited the priority of another
;;;4207                * task? */
;;;4208               if( pxTCB->uxPriority != pxTCB->uxBasePriority )
000010  6ce1              LDR      r1,[r4,#0x4c]
000012  6ae0              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x2c]
000014  4288              CMP      r0,r1
000016  d027              BEQ      |L58.104|
;;;4209               {
;;;4210                   /* Only disinherit if no other mutexes are held. */
;;;4211                   if( pxTCB->uxMutexesHeld == ( UBaseType_t ) 0 )
000018  6d20              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x50]
00001a  bb28              CBNZ     r0,|L58.104|
;;;4212                   {
;;;4213                       /* A task can only have an inherited priority if it holds
;;;4214                        * the mutex.  If the mutex is held by a task then it cannot be
;;;4215                        * given from an interrupt, and if a mutex is given by the
;;;4216                        * holding task then it must be the running state task.  Remove
;;;4217                        * the holding task from the ready list. */
;;;4218                       if( uxListRemove( &( pxTCB->xStateListItem ) ) == ( UBaseType_t ) 0 )
00001c  1d20              ADDS     r0,r4,#4
00001e  f7fffffe          BL       uxListRemove
000022  b940              CBNZ     r0,|L58.54|
;;;4219                       {
;;;4220                           portRESET_READY_PRIORITY( pxTCB->uxPriority, uxTopReadyPriority );
000024  f894102c          LDRB     r1,[r4,#0x2c]
000028  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
00002a  4088              LSLS     r0,r0,r1
00002c  490f              LDR      r1,|L58.108|
00002e  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
000030  4381              BICS     r1,r1,r0
000032  480e              LDR      r0,|L58.108|
000034  6001              STR      r1,[r0,#0]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
                  |L58.54|
;;;4221                       }
;;;4222                       else
;;;4223                       {
;;;4224                           mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;4225                       }
;;;4226   
;;;4227                       /* Disinherit the priority before adding the task into the
;;;4228                        * new  ready list. */
;;;4229                       traceTASK_PRIORITY_DISINHERIT( pxTCB, pxTCB->uxBasePriority );
;;;4230                       pxTCB->uxPriority = pxTCB->uxBasePriority;
000036  6ce0              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x4c]
000038  62e0              STR      r0,[r4,#0x2c]
;;;4231   
;;;4232                       /* Reset the event list item value.  It cannot be in use for
;;;4233                        * any other purpose if this task is running, and it must be
;;;4234                        * running to give back the mutex. */
;;;4235                       listSET_LIST_ITEM_VALUE( &( pxTCB->xEventListItem ), ( TickType_t ) configMAX_PRIORITIES - ( TickType_t ) pxTCB->uxPriority ); /*lint !e961 MISRA exception as the casts are only redundant for some ports. */
00003a  6ae0              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x2c]
00003c  f1c0000f          RSB      r0,r0,#0xf
000040  61a0              STR      r0,[r4,#0x18]
;;;4236                       prvAddTaskToReadyList( pxTCB );
000042  f894102c          LDRB     r1,[r4,#0x2c]
000046  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
000048  4088              LSLS     r0,r0,r1
00004a  4908              LDR      r1,|L58.108|
00004c  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
00004e  4308              ORRS     r0,r0,r1
000050  4906              LDR      r1,|L58.108|
000052  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
000054  6ae1              LDR      r1,[r4,#0x2c]
000056  eb010181          ADD      r1,r1,r1,LSL #2
00005a  4a05              LDR      r2,|L58.112|
00005c  eb020081          ADD      r0,r2,r1,LSL #2
000060  1d21              ADDS     r1,r4,#4
000062  f7fffffe          BL       vListInsertEnd
;;;4237   
;;;4238                       /* Return true to indicate that a context switch is required.
;;;4239                        * This is only actually required in the corner case whereby
;;;4240                        * multiple mutexes were held and the mutexes were given back
;;;4241                        * in an order different to that in which they were taken.
;;;4242                        * If a context switch did not occur when the first mutex was
;;;4243                        * returned, even if a task was waiting on it, then a context
;;;4244                        * switch should occur when the last mutex is returned whether
;;;4245                        * a task is waiting on it or not. */
;;;4246                       xReturn = pdTRUE;
000066  2601              MOVS     r6,#1
                  |L58.104|
;;;4247                   }
;;;4248                   else
;;;4249                   {
;;;4250                       mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;4251                   }
;;;4252               }
;;;4253               else
;;;4254               {
;;;4255                   mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;4256               }
;;;4257           }
;;;4258           else
;;;4259           {
;;;4260               mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;4261           }
;;;4262   
;;;4263           return xReturn;
000068  4630              MOV      r0,r6
;;;4264       }
00006a  bd70              POP      {r4-r6,pc}
;;;4265   
                          ENDP

                  |L58.108|
                          DCD      uxTopReadyPriority
                  |L58.112|
                          DCD      pxReadyTasksLists

                          AREA ||i.xTaskPriorityInherit||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  xTaskPriorityInherit PROC
;;;4100   
;;;4101       BaseType_t xTaskPriorityInherit( TaskHandle_t const pxMutexHolder )
000000  b570              PUSH     {r4-r6,lr}
;;;4102       {
000002  4605              MOV      r5,r0
;;;4103           TCB_t * const pxMutexHolderTCB = pxMutexHolder;
000004  462c              MOV      r4,r5
;;;4104           BaseType_t xReturn = pdFALSE;
000006  2600              MOVS     r6,#0
;;;4105   
;;;4106           /* If the mutex was given back by an interrupt while the queue was
;;;4107            * locked then the mutex holder might now be NULL.  _RB_ Is this still
;;;4108            * needed as interrupts can no longer use mutexes? */
;;;4109           if( pxMutexHolder != NULL )
000008  2d00              CMP      r5,#0
00000a  d04d              BEQ      |L59.168|
;;;4110           {
;;;4111               /* If the holder of the mutex has a priority below the priority of
;;;4112                * the task attempting to obtain the mutex then it will temporarily
;;;4113                * inherit the priority of the task attempting to obtain the mutex. */
;;;4114               if( pxMutexHolderTCB->uxPriority < pxCurrentTCB->uxPriority )
00000c  4927              LDR      r1,|L59.172|
00000e  6ae0              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x2c]
000010  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000012  6ac9              LDR      r1,[r1,#0x2c]
000014  4288              CMP      r0,r1
000016  d240              BCS      |L59.154|
;;;4115               {
;;;4116                   /* Adjust the mutex holder state to account for its new
;;;4117                    * priority.  Only reset the event list item value if the value is
;;;4118                    * not being used for anything else. */
;;;4119                   if( ( listGET_LIST_ITEM_VALUE( &( pxMutexHolderTCB->xEventListItem ) ) & taskEVENT_LIST_ITEM_VALUE_IN_USE ) == 0UL )
000018  69a0              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x18]
00001a  f0004000          AND      r0,r0,#0x80000000
00001e  b928              CBNZ     r0,|L59.44|
;;;4120                   {
;;;4121                       listSET_LIST_ITEM_VALUE( &( pxMutexHolderTCB->xEventListItem ), ( TickType_t ) configMAX_PRIORITIES - ( TickType_t ) pxCurrentTCB->uxPriority ); /*lint !e961 MISRA exception as the casts are only redundant for some ports. */
000020  4822              LDR      r0,|L59.172|
000022  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000024  6ac0              LDR      r0,[r0,#0x2c]
000026  f1c0000f          RSB      r0,r0,#0xf
00002a  61a0              STR      r0,[r4,#0x18]
                  |L59.44|
;;;4122                   }
;;;4123                   else
;;;4124                   {
;;;4125                       mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;4126                   }
;;;4127   
;;;4128                   /* If the task being modified is in the ready state it will need
;;;4129                    * to be moved into a new list. */
;;;4130                   if( listIS_CONTAINED_WITHIN( &( pxReadyTasksLists[ pxMutexHolderTCB->uxPriority ] ), &( pxMutexHolderTCB->xStateListItem ) ) != pdFALSE )
00002c  6ae1              LDR      r1,[r4,#0x2c]
00002e  eb010181          ADD      r1,r1,r1,LSL #2
000032  4a1f              LDR      r2,|L59.176|
000034  eb020181          ADD      r1,r2,r1,LSL #2
000038  6960              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x14]
00003a  4288              CMP      r0,r1
00003c  d101              BNE      |L59.66|
00003e  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
000040  e000              B        |L59.68|
                  |L59.66|
000042  2000              MOVS     r0,#0
                  |L59.68|
000044  b318              CBZ      r0,|L59.142|
;;;4131                   {
;;;4132                       if( uxListRemove( &( pxMutexHolderTCB->xStateListItem ) ) == ( UBaseType_t ) 0 )
000046  1d20              ADDS     r0,r4,#4
000048  f7fffffe          BL       uxListRemove
00004c  b940              CBNZ     r0,|L59.96|
;;;4133                       {
;;;4134                           /* It is known that the task is in its ready list so
;;;4135                            * there is no need to check again and the port level
;;;4136                            * reset macro can be called directly. */
;;;4137                           portRESET_READY_PRIORITY( pxMutexHolderTCB->uxPriority, uxTopReadyPriority );
00004e  f894102c          LDRB     r1,[r4,#0x2c]
000052  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
000054  4088              LSLS     r0,r0,r1
000056  4917              LDR      r1,|L59.180|
000058  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
00005a  4381              BICS     r1,r1,r0
00005c  4815              LDR      r0,|L59.180|
00005e  6001              STR      r1,[r0,#0]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
                  |L59.96|
;;;4138                       }
;;;4139                       else
;;;4140                       {
;;;4141                           mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;4142                       }
;;;4143   
;;;4144                       /* Inherit the priority before being moved into the new list. */
;;;4145                       pxMutexHolderTCB->uxPriority = pxCurrentTCB->uxPriority;
000060  4812              LDR      r0,|L59.172|
000062  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000064  6ac0              LDR      r0,[r0,#0x2c]
000066  62e0              STR      r0,[r4,#0x2c]
;;;4146                       prvAddTaskToReadyList( pxMutexHolderTCB );
000068  f894102c          LDRB     r1,[r4,#0x2c]
00006c  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
00006e  4088              LSLS     r0,r0,r1
000070  4910              LDR      r1,|L59.180|
000072  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
000074  4308              ORRS     r0,r0,r1
000076  490f              LDR      r1,|L59.180|
000078  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
00007a  6ae1              LDR      r1,[r4,#0x2c]
00007c  eb010181          ADD      r1,r1,r1,LSL #2
000080  4a0b              LDR      r2,|L59.176|
000082  eb020081          ADD      r0,r2,r1,LSL #2
000086  1d21              ADDS     r1,r4,#4
000088  f7fffffe          BL       vListInsertEnd
00008c  e003              B        |L59.150|
                  |L59.142|
;;;4147                   }
;;;4148                   else
;;;4149                   {
;;;4150                       /* Just inherit the priority. */
;;;4151                       pxMutexHolderTCB->uxPriority = pxCurrentTCB->uxPriority;
00008e  4807              LDR      r0,|L59.172|
000090  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000092  6ac0              LDR      r0,[r0,#0x2c]
000094  62e0              STR      r0,[r4,#0x2c]
                  |L59.150|
;;;4152                   }
;;;4153   
;;;4154                   traceTASK_PRIORITY_INHERIT( pxMutexHolderTCB, pxCurrentTCB->uxPriority );
;;;4155   
;;;4156                   /* Inheritance occurred. */
;;;4157                   xReturn = pdTRUE;
000096  2601              MOVS     r6,#1
000098  e006              B        |L59.168|
                  |L59.154|
;;;4158               }
;;;4159               else
;;;4160               {
;;;4161                   if( pxMutexHolderTCB->uxBasePriority < pxCurrentTCB->uxPriority )
00009a  4904              LDR      r1,|L59.172|
00009c  6ce0              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x4c]
00009e  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
0000a0  6ac9              LDR      r1,[r1,#0x2c]
0000a2  4288              CMP      r0,r1
0000a4  d200              BCS      |L59.168|
;;;4162                   {
;;;4163                       /* The base priority of the mutex holder is lower than the
;;;4164                        * priority of the task attempting to take the mutex, but the
;;;4165                        * current priority of the mutex holder is not lower than the
;;;4166                        * priority of the task attempting to take the mutex.
;;;4167                        * Therefore the mutex holder must have already inherited a
;;;4168                        * priority, but inheritance would have occurred if that had
;;;4169                        * not been the case. */
;;;4170                       xReturn = pdTRUE;
0000a6  2601              MOVS     r6,#1
                  |L59.168|
;;;4171                   }
;;;4172                   else
;;;4173                   {
;;;4174                       mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;4175                   }
;;;4176               }
;;;4177           }
;;;4178           else
;;;4179           {
;;;4180               mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;4181           }
;;;4182   
;;;4183           return xReturn;
0000a8  4630              MOV      r0,r6
;;;4184       }
0000aa  bd70              POP      {r4-r6,pc}
;;;4185   
                          ENDP

                  |L59.172|
                          DCD      pxCurrentTCB
                  |L59.176|
                          DCD      pxReadyTasksLists
                  |L59.180|
                          DCD      uxTopReadyPriority

                          AREA ||i.xTaskRemoveFromEventList||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  xTaskRemoveFromEventList PROC
;;;3209   
;;;3210   BaseType_t xTaskRemoveFromEventList( const List_t * const pxEventList )
000000  b570              PUSH     {r4-r6,lr}
;;;3211   {
000002  4606              MOV      r6,r0
;;;3212       TCB_t * pxUnblockedTCB;
;;;3213       BaseType_t xReturn;
;;;3214   
;;;3215       /* THIS FUNCTION MUST BE CALLED FROM A CRITICAL SECTION.  It can also be
;;;3216        * called from a critical section within an ISR. */
;;;3217   
;;;3218       /* The event list is sorted in priority order, so the first in the list can
;;;3219        * be removed as it is known to be the highest priority.  Remove the TCB from
;;;3220        * the delayed list, and add it to the ready list.
;;;3221        *
;;;3222        * If an event is for a queue that is locked then this function will never
;;;3223        * get called - the lock count on the queue will get modified instead.  This
;;;3224        * means exclusive access to the event list is guaranteed here.
;;;3225        *
;;;3226        * This function assumes that a check has already been made to ensure that
;;;3227        * pxEventList is not empty. */
;;;3228       pxUnblockedTCB = listGET_OWNER_OF_HEAD_ENTRY( pxEventList ); /*lint !e9079 void * is used as this macro is used with timers and co-routines too.  Alignment is known to be fine as the type of the pointer stored and retrieved is the same. */
000004  68f0              LDR      r0,[r6,#0xc]
000006  68c4              LDR      r4,[r0,#0xc]
;;;3229       configASSERT( pxUnblockedTCB );
;;;3230       ( void ) uxListRemove( &( pxUnblockedTCB->xEventListItem ) );
000008  f1040018          ADD      r0,r4,#0x18
00000c  f7fffffe          BL       uxListRemove
;;;3231   
;;;3232       if( uxSchedulerSuspended == ( UBaseType_t ) pdFALSE )
000010  4815              LDR      r0,|L60.104|
000012  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; uxSchedulerSuspended
000014  b9a8              CBNZ     r0,|L60.66|
;;;3233       {
;;;3234           ( void ) uxListRemove( &( pxUnblockedTCB->xStateListItem ) );
000016  1d20              ADDS     r0,r4,#4
000018  f7fffffe          BL       uxListRemove
;;;3235           prvAddTaskToReadyList( pxUnblockedTCB );
00001c  f894102c          LDRB     r1,[r4,#0x2c]
000020  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
000022  4088              LSLS     r0,r0,r1
000024  4911              LDR      r1,|L60.108|
000026  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
000028  4308              ORRS     r0,r0,r1
00002a  4910              LDR      r1,|L60.108|
00002c  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
00002e  6ae1              LDR      r1,[r4,#0x2c]
000030  eb010181          ADD      r1,r1,r1,LSL #2
000034  4a0e              LDR      r2,|L60.112|
000036  eb020081          ADD      r0,r2,r1,LSL #2
00003a  1d21              ADDS     r1,r4,#4
00003c  f7fffffe          BL       vListInsertEnd
000040  e004              B        |L60.76|
                  |L60.66|
;;;3236   
;;;3237           #if ( configUSE_TICKLESS_IDLE != 0 )
;;;3238               {
;;;3239                   /* If a task is blocked on a kernel object then xNextTaskUnblockTime
;;;3240                    * might be set to the blocked task's time out time.  If the task is
;;;3241                    * unblocked for a reason other than a timeout xNextTaskUnblockTime is
;;;3242                    * normally left unchanged, because it is automatically reset to a new
;;;3243                    * value when the tick count equals xNextTaskUnblockTime.  However if
;;;3244                    * tickless idling is used it might be more important to enter sleep mode
;;;3245                    * at the earliest possible time - so reset xNextTaskUnblockTime here to
;;;3246                    * ensure it is updated at the earliest possible time. */
;;;3247                   prvResetNextTaskUnblockTime();
;;;3248               }
;;;3249           #endif
;;;3250       }
;;;3251       else
;;;3252       {
;;;3253           /* The delayed and ready lists cannot be accessed, so hold this task
;;;3254            * pending until the scheduler is resumed. */
;;;3255           vListInsertEnd( &( xPendingReadyList ), &( pxUnblockedTCB->xEventListItem ) );
000042  f1040118          ADD      r1,r4,#0x18
000046  480b              LDR      r0,|L60.116|
000048  f7fffffe          BL       vListInsertEnd
                  |L60.76|
;;;3256       }
;;;3257   
;;;3258       if( pxUnblockedTCB->uxPriority > pxCurrentTCB->uxPriority )
00004c  490a              LDR      r1,|L60.120|
00004e  6ae0              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x2c]
000050  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000052  6ac9              LDR      r1,[r1,#0x2c]
000054  4288              CMP      r0,r1
000056  d904              BLS      |L60.98|
;;;3259       {
;;;3260           /* Return true if the task removed from the event list has a higher
;;;3261            * priority than the calling task.  This allows the calling task to know if
;;;3262            * it should force a context switch now. */
;;;3263           xReturn = pdTRUE;
000058  2501              MOVS     r5,#1
;;;3264   
;;;3265           /* Mark that a yield is pending in case the user is not using the
;;;3266            * "xHigherPriorityTaskWoken" parameter to an ISR safe FreeRTOS function. */
;;;3267           xYieldPending = pdTRUE;
00005a  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
00005c  4907              LDR      r1,|L60.124|
00005e  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; xYieldPending
000060  e000              B        |L60.100|
                  |L60.98|
;;;3268       }
;;;3269       else
;;;3270       {
;;;3271           xReturn = pdFALSE;
000062  2500              MOVS     r5,#0
                  |L60.100|
;;;3272       }
;;;3273   
;;;3274       return xReturn;
000064  4628              MOV      r0,r5
;;;3275   }
000066  bd70              POP      {r4-r6,pc}
;;;3276   /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
                          ENDP

                  |L60.104|
                          DCD      uxSchedulerSuspended
                  |L60.108|
                          DCD      uxTopReadyPriority
                  |L60.112|
                          DCD      pxReadyTasksLists
                  |L60.116|
                          DCD      xPendingReadyList
                  |L60.120|
                          DCD      pxCurrentTCB
                  |L60.124|
                          DCD      xYieldPending

                          AREA ||i.xTaskResumeAll||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  xTaskResumeAll PROC
;;;2254   
;;;2255   BaseType_t xTaskResumeAll( void )
000000  b570              PUSH     {r4-r6,lr}
;;;2256   {
;;;2257       TCB_t * pxTCB = NULL;
000002  2400              MOVS     r4,#0
;;;2258       BaseType_t xAlreadyYielded = pdFALSE;
000004  2600              MOVS     r6,#0
;;;2259   
;;;2260       /* If uxSchedulerSuspended is zero then this function does not match a
;;;2261        * previous call to vTaskSuspendAll(). */
;;;2262       configASSERT( uxSchedulerSuspended );
;;;2263   
;;;2264       /* It is possible that an ISR caused a task to be removed from an event
;;;2265        * list while the scheduler was suspended.  If this was the case then the
;;;2266        * removed task will have been added to the xPendingReadyList.  Once the
;;;2267        * scheduler has been resumed it is safe to move all the pending ready
;;;2268        * tasks from this list into their appropriate ready list. */
;;;2269       taskENTER_CRITICAL();
000006  f7fffffe          BL       vPortEnterCritical
;;;2270       {
;;;2271           --uxSchedulerSuspended;
00000a  482e              LDR      r0,|L61.196|
00000c  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; uxSchedulerSuspended
00000e  1e40              SUBS     r0,r0,#1
000010  492c              LDR      r1,|L61.196|
000012  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; uxSchedulerSuspended
;;;2272   
;;;2273           if( uxSchedulerSuspended == ( UBaseType_t ) pdFALSE )
000014  4608              MOV      r0,r1
000016  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; uxSchedulerSuspended
000018  2800              CMP      r0,#0
00001a  d14f              BNE      |L61.188|
;;;2274           {
;;;2275               if( uxCurrentNumberOfTasks > ( UBaseType_t ) 0U )
00001c  482a              LDR      r0,|L61.200|
00001e  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; uxCurrentNumberOfTasks
000020  2800              CMP      r0,#0
000022  d04b              BEQ      |L61.188|
;;;2276               {
;;;2277                   /* Move any readied tasks from the pending list into the
;;;2278                    * appropriate ready list. */
;;;2279                   while( listLIST_IS_EMPTY( &xPendingReadyList ) == pdFALSE )
000024  e024              B        |L61.112|
                  |L61.38|
;;;2280                   {
;;;2281                       pxTCB = listGET_OWNER_OF_HEAD_ENTRY( ( &xPendingReadyList ) ); /*lint !e9079 void * is used as this macro is used with timers and co-routines too.  Alignment is known to be fine as the type of the pointer stored and retrieved is the same. */
000026  4829              LDR      r0,|L61.204|
000028  68c0              LDR      r0,[r0,#0xc]
00002a  68c4              LDR      r4,[r0,#0xc]
;;;2282                       ( void ) uxListRemove( &( pxTCB->xEventListItem ) );
00002c  f1040018          ADD      r0,r4,#0x18
000030  f7fffffe          BL       uxListRemove
;;;2283                       ( void ) uxListRemove( &( pxTCB->xStateListItem ) );
000034  1d20              ADDS     r0,r4,#4
000036  f7fffffe          BL       uxListRemove
;;;2284                       prvAddTaskToReadyList( pxTCB );
00003a  f894102c          LDRB     r1,[r4,#0x2c]
00003e  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
000040  4088              LSLS     r0,r0,r1
000042  4923              LDR      r1,|L61.208|
000044  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
000046  4308              ORRS     r0,r0,r1
000048  4921              LDR      r1,|L61.208|
00004a  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
00004c  6ae1              LDR      r1,[r4,#0x2c]
00004e  eb010181          ADD      r1,r1,r1,LSL #2
000052  4a20              LDR      r2,|L61.212|
000054  eb020081          ADD      r0,r2,r1,LSL #2
000058  1d21              ADDS     r1,r4,#4
00005a  f7fffffe          BL       vListInsertEnd
;;;2285   
;;;2286                       /* If the moved task has a priority higher than the current
;;;2287                        * task then a yield must be performed. */
;;;2288                       if( pxTCB->uxPriority >= pxCurrentTCB->uxPriority )
00005e  491e              LDR      r1,|L61.216|
000060  6ae0              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x2c]
000062  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000064  6ac9              LDR      r1,[r1,#0x2c]
000066  4288              CMP      r0,r1
000068  d302              BCC      |L61.112|
;;;2289                       {
;;;2290                           xYieldPending = pdTRUE;
00006a  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
00006c  491b              LDR      r1,|L61.220|
00006e  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; xYieldPending
                  |L61.112|
000070  4816              LDR      r0,|L61.204|
000072  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]            ;2279  ; xPendingReadyList
000074  b908              CBNZ     r0,|L61.122|
000076  2001              MOVS     r0,#1                 ;2279
000078  e000              B        |L61.124|
                  |L61.122|
00007a  2000              MOVS     r0,#0                 ;2279
                  |L61.124|
00007c  2800              CMP      r0,#0                 ;2279
00007e  d0d2              BEQ      |L61.38|
;;;2291                       }
;;;2292                       else
;;;2293                       {
;;;2294                           mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;2295                       }
;;;2296                   }
;;;2297   
;;;2298                   if( pxTCB != NULL )
000080  b10c              CBZ      r4,|L61.134|
;;;2299                   {
;;;2300                       /* A task was unblocked while the scheduler was suspended,
;;;2301                        * which may have prevented the next unblock time from being
;;;2302                        * re-calculated, in which case re-calculate it now.  Mainly
;;;2303                        * important for low power tickless implementations, where
;;;2304                        * this can prevent an unnecessary exit from low power
;;;2305                        * state. */
;;;2306                       prvResetNextTaskUnblockTime();
000082  f7fffffe          BL       prvResetNextTaskUnblockTime
                  |L61.134|
;;;2307                   }
;;;2308   
;;;2309                   /* If any ticks occurred while the scheduler was suspended then
;;;2310                    * they should be processed now.  This ensures the tick count does
;;;2311                    * not  slip, and that any delayed tasks are resumed at the correct
;;;2312                    * time. */
;;;2313                   {
;;;2314                       TickType_t xPendedCounts = xPendedTicks; /* Non-volatile copy. */
000086  4816              LDR      r0,|L61.224|
000088  6805              LDR      r5,[r0,#0]  ; xPendedTicks
;;;2315   
;;;2316                       if( xPendedCounts > ( TickType_t ) 0U )
00008a  b165              CBZ      r5,|L61.166|
;;;2317                       {
;;;2318                           do
00008c  bf00              NOP      
                  |L61.142|
;;;2319                           {
;;;2320                               if( xTaskIncrementTick() != pdFALSE )
00008e  f7fffffe          BL       xTaskIncrementTick
000092  b110              CBZ      r0,|L61.154|
;;;2321                               {
;;;2322                                   xYieldPending = pdTRUE;
000094  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
000096  4911              LDR      r1,|L61.220|
000098  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; xYieldPending
                  |L61.154|
;;;2323                               }
;;;2324                               else
;;;2325                               {
;;;2326                                   mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;2327                               }
;;;2328   
;;;2329                               --xPendedCounts;
00009a  1e6d              SUBS     r5,r5,#1
;;;2330                           } while( xPendedCounts > ( TickType_t ) 0U );
00009c  2d00              CMP      r5,#0
00009e  d1f6              BNE      |L61.142|
;;;2331   
;;;2332                           xPendedTicks = 0;
0000a0  2000              MOVS     r0,#0
0000a2  490f              LDR      r1,|L61.224|
0000a4  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; xPendedTicks
                  |L61.166|
;;;2333                       }
;;;2334                       else
;;;2335                       {
;;;2336                           mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;2337                       }
;;;2338                   }
;;;2339   
;;;2340                   if( xYieldPending != pdFALSE )
0000a6  480d              LDR      r0,|L61.220|
0000a8  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; xYieldPending
0000aa  b138              CBZ      r0,|L61.188|
;;;2341                   {
;;;2342                       #if ( configUSE_PREEMPTION != 0 )
;;;2343                           {
;;;2344                               xAlreadyYielded = pdTRUE;
0000ac  2601              MOVS     r6,#1
;;;2345                           }
;;;2346                       #endif
;;;2347                       taskYIELD_IF_USING_PREEMPTION();
0000ae  0730              LSLS     r0,r6,#28
0000b0  490c              LDR      r1,|L61.228|
0000b2  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]
0000b4  f3bf8f4f          DSB      
0000b8  f3bf8f6f          ISB      
                  |L61.188|
;;;2348                   }
;;;2349                   else
;;;2350                   {
;;;2351                       mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;2352                   }
;;;2353               }
;;;2354           }
;;;2355           else
;;;2356           {
;;;2357               mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;2358           }
;;;2359       }
;;;2360       taskEXIT_CRITICAL();
0000bc  f7fffffe          BL       vPortExitCritical
;;;2361   
;;;2362       return xAlreadyYielded;
0000c0  4630              MOV      r0,r6
;;;2363   }
0000c2  bd70              POP      {r4-r6,pc}
;;;2364   /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
                          ENDP

                  |L61.196|
                          DCD      uxSchedulerSuspended
                  |L61.200|
                          DCD      uxCurrentNumberOfTasks
                  |L61.204|
                          DCD      xPendingReadyList
                  |L61.208|
                          DCD      uxTopReadyPriority
                  |L61.212|
                          DCD      pxReadyTasksLists
                  |L61.216|
                          DCD      pxCurrentTCB
                  |L61.220|
                          DCD      xYieldPending
                  |L61.224|
                          DCD      xPendedTicks
                  |L61.228|
                          DCD      0xe000ed04

                          AREA ||i.xTaskResumeFromISR||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  xTaskResumeFromISR PROC
;;;1956   
;;;1957       BaseType_t xTaskResumeFromISR( TaskHandle_t xTaskToResume )
000000  e92d41f0          PUSH     {r4-r8,lr}
;;;1958       {
000004  4606              MOV      r6,r0
;;;1959           BaseType_t xYieldRequired = pdFALSE;
000006  2700              MOVS     r7,#0
;;;1960           TCB_t * const pxTCB = xTaskToResume;
000008  4634              MOV      r4,r6
;;;1961           UBaseType_t uxSavedInterruptStatus;
;;;1962   
;;;1963           configASSERT( xTaskToResume );
;;;1964   
;;;1965           /* RTOS ports that support interrupt nesting have the concept of a
;;;1966            * maximum  system call (or maximum API call) interrupt priority.
;;;1967            * Interrupts that are  above the maximum system call priority are keep
;;;1968            * permanently enabled, even when the RTOS kernel is in a critical section,
;;;1969            * but cannot make any calls to FreeRTOS API functions.  If configASSERT()
;;;1970            * is defined in FreeRTOSConfig.h then
;;;1971            * portASSERT_IF_INTERRUPT_PRIORITY_INVALID() will result in an assertion
;;;1972            * failure if a FreeRTOS API function is called from an interrupt that has
;;;1973            * been assigned a priority above the configured maximum system call
;;;1974            * priority.  Only FreeRTOS functions that end in FromISR can be called
;;;1975            * from interrupts  that have been assigned a priority at or (logically)
;;;1976            * below the maximum system call interrupt priority.  FreeRTOS maintains a
;;;1977            * separate interrupt safe API to ensure interrupt entry is as fast and as
;;;1978            * simple as possible.  More information (albeit Cortex-M specific) is
;;;1979            * provided on the following link:
;;;1980            * https://www.FreeRTOS.org/RTOS-Cortex-M3-M4.html */
;;;1981           portASSERT_IF_INTERRUPT_PRIORITY_INVALID();
;;;1982   
;;;1983           uxSavedInterruptStatus = portSET_INTERRUPT_MASK_FROM_ISR();
00000a  bf00              NOP      
00000c  21bf              MOVS     r1,#0xbf
00000e  f3ef8011          MRS      r0,BASEPRI
000012  f3818811          MSR      BASEPRI,r1
000016  f3bf8f4f          DSB      
00001a  f3bf8f6f          ISB      
00001e  bf00              NOP      
000020  4605              MOV      r5,r0
;;;1984           {
;;;1985               if( prvTaskIsTaskSuspended( pxTCB ) != pdFALSE )
000022  4620              MOV      r0,r4
000024  f7fffffe          BL       prvTaskIsTaskSuspended
000028  b308              CBZ      r0,|L62.110|
;;;1986               {
;;;1987                   traceTASK_RESUME_FROM_ISR( pxTCB );
;;;1988   
;;;1989                   /* Check the ready lists can be accessed. */
;;;1990                   if( uxSchedulerSuspended == ( UBaseType_t ) pdFALSE )
00002a  4817              LDR      r0,|L62.136|
00002c  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; uxSchedulerSuspended
00002e  b9f8              CBNZ     r0,|L62.112|
;;;1991                   {
;;;1992                       /* Ready lists can be accessed so move the task from the
;;;1993                        * suspended list to the ready list directly. */
;;;1994                       if( pxTCB->uxPriority >= pxCurrentTCB->uxPriority )
000030  4916              LDR      r1,|L62.140|
000032  6ae0              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x2c]
000034  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000036  6ac9              LDR      r1,[r1,#0x2c]
000038  4288              CMP      r0,r1
00003a  d303              BCC      |L62.68|
;;;1995                       {
;;;1996                           xYieldRequired = pdTRUE;
00003c  2701              MOVS     r7,#1
;;;1997   
;;;1998                           /* Mark that a yield is pending in case the user is not
;;;1999                            * using the return value to initiate a context switch
;;;2000                            * from the ISR using portYIELD_FROM_ISR. */
;;;2001                           xYieldPending = pdTRUE;
00003e  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
000040  4913              LDR      r1,|L62.144|
000042  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; xYieldPending
                  |L62.68|
;;;2002                       }
;;;2003                       else
;;;2004                       {
;;;2005                           mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;2006                       }
;;;2007   
;;;2008                       ( void ) uxListRemove( &( pxTCB->xStateListItem ) );
000044  1d20              ADDS     r0,r4,#4
000046  f7fffffe          BL       uxListRemove
;;;2009                       prvAddTaskToReadyList( pxTCB );
00004a  f894102c          LDRB     r1,[r4,#0x2c]
00004e  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
000050  4088              LSLS     r0,r0,r1
000052  4910              LDR      r1,|L62.148|
000054  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
000056  4308              ORRS     r0,r0,r1
000058  490e              LDR      r1,|L62.148|
00005a  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
00005c  6ae1              LDR      r1,[r4,#0x2c]
00005e  eb010181          ADD      r1,r1,r1,LSL #2
000062  4a0d              LDR      r2,|L62.152|
000064  eb020081          ADD      r0,r2,r1,LSL #2
000068  1d21              ADDS     r1,r4,#4
00006a  f7fffffe          BL       vListInsertEnd
                  |L62.110|
00006e  e004              B        |L62.122|
                  |L62.112|
;;;2010                   }
;;;2011                   else
;;;2012                   {
;;;2013                       /* The delayed or ready lists cannot be accessed so the task
;;;2014                        * is held in the pending ready list until the scheduler is
;;;2015                        * unsuspended. */
;;;2016                       vListInsertEnd( &( xPendingReadyList ), &( pxTCB->xEventListItem ) );
000070  f1040118          ADD      r1,r4,#0x18
000074  4809              LDR      r0,|L62.156|
000076  f7fffffe          BL       vListInsertEnd
                  |L62.122|
;;;2017                   }
;;;2018               }
;;;2019               else
;;;2020               {
;;;2021                   mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;2022               }
;;;2023           }
;;;2024           portCLEAR_INTERRUPT_MASK_FROM_ISR( uxSavedInterruptStatus );
00007a  bf00              NOP      
00007c  f3858811          MSR      BASEPRI,r5
000080  bf00              NOP      
;;;2025   
;;;2026           return xYieldRequired;
000082  4638              MOV      r0,r7
;;;2027       }
000084  e8bd81f0          POP      {r4-r8,pc}
;;;2028   
                          ENDP

                  |L62.136|
                          DCD      uxSchedulerSuspended
                  |L62.140|
                          DCD      pxCurrentTCB
                  |L62.144|
                          DCD      xYieldPending
                  |L62.148|
                          DCD      uxTopReadyPriority
                  |L62.152|
                          DCD      pxReadyTasksLists
                  |L62.156|
                          DCD      xPendingReadyList

                          AREA ||.bss||, DATA, NOINIT, ALIGN=2

                  pxReadyTasksLists
                          %        300
                  xDelayedTaskList1
                          %        20
                  xDelayedTaskList2
                          %        20
                  xPendingReadyList

                  xSuspendedTaskList
                          %        20

                          AREA ||.data||, DATA, ALIGN=2

                  pxCurrentTCB
                          DCD      0x00000000
                  pxDelayedTaskList
                          DCD      0x00000000
                  pxOverflowDelayedTaskList
                          DCD      0x00000000
                  uxDeletedTasksWaitingCleanUp
                          DCD      0x00000000
                  uxCurrentNumberOfTasks
                          DCD      0x00000000
                  xTickCount
                          DCD      0x00000000
                  uxTopReadyPriority
                          DCD      0x00000000
                  xSchedulerRunning
                          DCD      0x00000000
                  xPendedTicks
                          DCD      0x00000000
                  xYieldPending
                          DCD      0x00000000
                  xNumOfOverflows
                          DCD      0x00000000
                  uxTaskNumber
                          DCD      0x00000000
                  xNextTaskUnblockTime
                          DCD      0x00000000
                  xIdleTaskHandle
                          DCD      0x00000000
                  uxTopUsedPriority
                          DCD      0x0000000e
                  uxSchedulerSuspended
                          DCD      0x00000000
                  ulTaskSwitchedInTime
                          DCD      0x00000000
                  ulTotalRunTime
                          DCD      0x00000000
